channel.title: Waiting To Be Signed
channel.description:
Waiting To Be Signed is a weekly podcast about generative art on the blockchain. Every week we release an episode covering new releases we love, the news & happenings of the space, hot topics & more. We also release bi-weekly interview episodes with artists, collectors and builders in the space.
If you want to support the show please check out our Patreon, https://www.patreon.com/WaitingToBeSigned or you can donate directly to wtbs.tez & wtbs.eth
channel.author: Willpop + Trinity
channel.itunes:author: Willpop + Trinity

Waiting To Be Signed is a weekly podcast about generative art on the blockchain. Every week we release an episode covering new releases we love, the news & happenings of the space, hot topics & more. We also release bi-weekly interview episodes with artists, collectors and builders in the space.
If you want to support the show please check out our Patreon, https://www.patreon.com/WaitingToBeSigned or you can donate directly to wtbs.tez & wtbs.eth

item.title: Turning Pokemon Cards into fx(hash) Grails - Interview with McLlama
item.description:
WTBS is back with a rare collector interview. In this episode Will is joined by longtime fx(hash) collector McLlama to talk about his recent grail buying spree!

Follow McLlama on Twitter @PompousLL

Check out his fantastic collection on Raster

In this episode we discuss:

  • McLlama's background in collecting and how he came to crypto and NFTs
  • Are 'early' collections still important?
  • Relevance of chain, Tezos FUD and the recent Eth FUD
  • The various pivots of fx(hash) over the years and $fxh/$art coins
  • Curated platforms and the consolidation of the ecosystem around a smaller number of players
  • Long-term conviction in crypto/Bitcoin
  • Pokemon TCG collecting, slabbing & flipping
  • Origin of the McLlama name & outro


Episode Art: Farbteiler #388 by Erik Swahn, owned by McLlama

Intro & outro tracks by PixelWank

item.title: Cave Painting In The Digital Age - Interview With Remnynt
item.description:
WTBS is back with another interview episode. We are pleased to be joined by Jimmy Griffith aka Remnynt to talk about his latest self-released work 'Architectonica'.

Follow Remnynt on Twitter @remnynt

Check out his amazing artist website, vibes.art

In this episode we discuss:

  • Remnynt's background in art, coding, game development, and how he discovered crypto & NFTs
  • Early appreciation for Terraforms and Loot and how those projects connect to his earliest generative releases
  • The experience of being one of the last big Art Blocks curated releases with Proscenium
  • Struggles with memory loss caused by an ongoing seizure disorder
  • The pressure to release Architectonica and his novel approach to collector curated
  • The financial outcome of Architectonica vs. expectation and what he would have done differently
  • The potential of expanding art beyond the blockchain to the full internet audience through printing services
  • Zelda, World of Warcraft, Diablo 2 & collecting niche game cartridges
  • Music and Magic: The Gathering!


Thanks for listening as always!

Intro & Outro tracks by Pixelwank

item.title: E125 - Going To College
item.description:
Will & Trinity convene once again to catch up on the latest announcement around fx(hash). In this episode we discuss:

  • fx(hash) suspended as of April 11
  • Everything we wish fx(hash) had developed at some point
  • Theoretical costs of running a platform by E L L A
  • Bootloader now supports p5 projects
  • Infinite Auction by Rudxane at auctions.rudxane.xyz
  • Compressed Nostalgia by Rudxane
  • Getting a cat & kid's movie discussions


Episode Art: The website message when Vercel bill was not

Intro & Outro tracks by PixelWank

item.title: Following The Line - Interview with CCDDBB (Claudio Dalla Bernardina)
item.description:
In this interview episode of Waiting to Be Signed, artist CCDDBB (Claudio Dalla Bernardina) recounts growing up in a small village in Italy, learning BASIC on a VIC-20 and later HTML/JavaScript, studying fine arts/media design in Milan, doing 3D work and photography, producing documentaries, then moving to Thailand to manage a restaurant. He describes how COVID shutdowns and his long involvement with crypto led him to Tezos NFTs, early activity on Hic Et Nunc, and then fx(hash), where he learned generative techniques through community peers. He discusses his evolving approach using “profiles” for meaningful rarity and his ongoing “Plotable Mess” series.


Follow Claudio on Twitter @CCDDBB

And on Instagram @CCDDBB.art

Check out his websites for more information about his work as well

ccddbb.vercel.app

https://www.claudiodallabernardina.com/


00:00 Welcome and Guest Intro

01:37 Early Computers and BASIC

04:11 Internet and First Web Work

06:00 Milan Fine Arts and 3D Studio

08:00 Photography and Reportage Lessons

09:47 Documentaries and Leaving Italy

12:13 Thailand Restaurant Reset

13:39 COVID and Crypto Comeback

16:40 Discovering NFTs and Hic Et Nunc

22:42 AI Side Projects and McFly Persona

26:34 fx(hash) Beginnings and First Drop

28:29 Generative Techniques and Community

30:40 Profiles, Rarity, and Project Craft

33:07 Breaking Power Ranges

35:07 One Line Obsession

36:44 Plotter Projects Stories

42:09 Why Not fx(hash)?

45:32 Plotting Process And Tools

47:34 Curated Release Plans

50:59 Bear Market Reflections

56:25 Blockchain Pros And Cons

01:02:10 Music And 'The Wallet'

01:08:41 Final Thanks And Goodbye


Thanks to Claudio for taking the time to come on the show!

Episode Art: one of many great Plottable Mess outputs by Claudio

Intro and Outro tracks by PixelWank

item.title: E124 - Choose Your Own Sports Analogy
item.description:
Will & Trinity are back after several months to recap and discuss everything going on with fx(hash). Hit us up in DMs etc with your thoughts.

In this episode we discuss:

  • Recapping where we were around our last episode in October
  • fx(hash)+, $plus, and Lonliboy being brought on to curate
  • Deliverance by Valent, the first curated release on fx(hash)
  • Theoretical Civilization by Studio Yorktown, pre-curated release
  • The success of Rayincarnations by Volatile Moods & where price point meets new expectations
  • The Emerge Fund
  • fx(hash) announced additional funding, roadmap for the future, and speculation around upcoming releases
  • fx(hash) 2026 Vision post from 2/16
  • Superpositions by William Mapan via Iconic Culture at Art Basel Paris
  • Sessions by Sara Ludy on Objkt via Office Impart & Anika Meier
  • https://www.editart.xyz/
  • bootloader.art


Thanks as always for listening. Back soonish, maybe.

Intro & outro tracks by Pixelwank

Episode Art: Rayincarnations #57 by Volatile Moods

item.title: Artist Commons: Art Whispering with Haiver
item.description:
WTBS is excited to welcome fellow New Yorker and long time friend Haiver onto the show. We've known Haiver from the NYC scene for years and have often talked about setting up an interview. In November Haiver announced the launch of Artist Commons, an educational platform for artists to level up their skills through lectures, feedback, mentorship and more!

Subscribe to their Email List to get program details and updates on Artist Commons

If you are interested to apply, please use our referral code: WTBS2026 to help support the show.

In this episode we discuss:

  • Haiver's background in lens-based art and how he came to discover generative art on the blockchain
  • The story of co-founding alba.art and what led him down the path to Artist Commons
  • What exactly is Artist Commons?
  • How (and why) this era of Generative Artists can connect with art history to better contextualize their work
  • What artists can expect from receiving, and giving, feedback as a part of the Artist Commons curriculum
  • What is the 'business of art'?
  • Future ideas for Artist Commons as a platform & the potential for Haiver to get back to curating!
  • How visual artists can build multiple revenue streams to sustain a career in art
  • How Alba, fx(hash) and now Artist Commons are striving to enable more artists to succeed
  • Artist Commons wrap-up & call for applications
  • Bonus Question: Nirvanna the Band the Show the Movie


Follow Haiver on Twitter @HaiverArt and check out his Linktree for more

Follow Artist Commons on Twitter @Artist_Commons and find them on Instagram, Farcaster etc under that same handle


Intro and Outro tracks by PixelWank

item.title: E123 - Go Camping!
item.description:
fx(hash) is moments away from throwing open the doors. $fxh is ripping while crypto markets are down. It's been ~3 months since our last numbered episode... Let's Go!

Will & Trinity are back to recap all things fx(hash) + a healthy dose of Verse releases. This is an XL episode, hope you enjoy.

NOTES

  • ArtBlocks 500 Announcement
  • AB Buys/Partners with TRAME
  • AB Going all in on generative objects?
  • Final 500 project, Quine by Larva Labs
  • SOLOS points on Verse
  • Verse says goodbye to Lonliboy & Sam
  • Highlight pivoting to smart contract vibe coding product... Vie.dev
  • OBJKT side project launches Bootloader to support generative art on Tezos
  • Le Random releases unified art marketplace Raster
  • SHVMBLDR is Free!
  • fx(hash) first quarterly report
  • fx(hash) reopening 10/22 announcement
  • Lot's of discussion about the slowdown in momentum and coms
  • Collecting strategies with $art tokens, $fxh and the 'refunding' strategy
  • Lot's of Tezos grails selling as Darold and others liquidate
  • Wishlist for Open Form projects!! Shout outs here include: Öz by DistCollective, Digitum Mens Trahit by David Seven, Traces by PixelSymphony & more
  • Residue by Mike Balzer via HEFT & Verse
  • Material Certainty and Material Violence by Jeres via Heft & Verse
  • Pheno by Julian Hespinheide on fx(hash) ($text)
  • Inner Forms by Ty Vek on fx(hash) ($vek)
  • check out the SLIPS website by Rudxane
  • an improbable thread and the thread we have by gozo via Artie Gallery & Verse
  • Zero (Broken Symmetry) by Eric De Giuli on Verse SOLOS
  • Shout out to HEFT for all the great releases big and small!
  • Soft Continuum by MCHX via Artie Gallery & Verse
  • Kevin Esherick kicks of the Artist is Absent series
  • CCDDBB showing amazing code/plotted series, 'Plottable Mess' all over socials


Thanks as always for listening.

Episode Art: 'Residue Fibers' from Residue by Mike Balzer

Intro & Outro tracks by PixelWank

item.title: Can't Live A Normal Life: Interview with Jammasterlee
item.description:
Welcome everyone to a special & long-in-the-making WTBS interview episode! Many listeners are probably not aware of Steven Lee aka Jammasterlee on tiktok & twitter. Steven leveraged his entire life on Bitcoin back in 2021 with the goal of retiring off the crypto bull market. He quit his job and put every dollar he could get his hands on into a leveraged Bitcoin position... and he put the whole thing on tiktok. Steven is now working on editing his videos together into a movie to encapsulate the phase of his life. I found Steven at the beginning of his story and have been watching ever since. Hope you all enjoy!

Follow Steven on Twitter @jammasterlee

Follow Steven on Tiktok @jammasterlee

Check out Steven's Substack as well!

Mentioned in the episode & very much worth viewing:

Double Fine Adventure! on Youtube

Double Fine PsychOdyssey on Youtube

Episode Art: Steve's Twitter PFP

Intro and Outro tracks by Pixelwank

item.title: Big Yapping about $fxh with Pronoia
item.description:
We have a special discussion episode this week to share with you. Long time friend of the show & former substitute co-host Pronoia is on to yap with us all about fx(hash), $fxh, $art coins & Open Form. This is a fantastic discussion that is very much Not Financial Advice!

  • Welcome back Pronoia & catching up on his last 18 months, including being hired & leaving Trilitech
  • fx(hash) decoupling from Tezos, the move to base, and overall review of the launch of the protocol.
  • What is the 'Flywheel' and do we believe in it?
  • Pronoia's current trading strategy with $fxh and $art coins.
  • Launching an $art coin without a project?
  • Advice to artists on how to manage an $art coin launch
  • Thoughts on bridging Tezos projects to Ethereum and how that could impact fx(hash) and the $fxh flywheel as a whole.


Thanks to Pronoia for taking the time to come on the show!

Follow Pronoia on Twitter @Pronoia10

Episode Art: Inner Forms #407 by Ty Vek (minted by Pronoia)

Intro and Outro tracks by PixelWank

item.title: E122 - Concern Trolling (Flywheel)
item.description:
Welcome back to another WTBS. Will and Trinity are here with a core episode to go over the first few weeks of Open Form and the new $fxh powered $art coin ecosystem. Short notes today for the sake of getting this out... in this episode we discuss:

  • $fxh launch and bots/snipers getting to the bonding curve
  • $art coin & project performance + pricing strategy observations
  • fx(hash) roadmap, the shift away from Tezos, and the potential for bridging Tezos art to Etherium
  • See all the projects discussed here (Open Form on fx(hash))
  • Participate in the Uniswap LP here if that interests you
  • upcoming release from Eric Andwer
  • SOMA by Erik Swahn on Verse
  • House Talk


Episode Art: m e t r o #160 by Punevyr

Intro & Outro tracks by PixelWank

item.title: 10 Artists Planning Open Form Releases on fx(hash)
item.description:
In this special 'call-in' episode of 'Waiting to be Signed,' host Will dives into the excitement surrounding fx(hash)'s new Open Form generative art platform and the launch of its token. This episode features 10 artists who generously took the time to record their response to three prompts:

1. Tell us about your project, what ideas/themes are you exploring here?

2. what was your experience like creating an Open Form project? How did you take into account the potential for large edition size? What about the generational/evolution aspect that is now enabled?,

3. How do you feel about releasing an $art coin as part of your project? What will your coin be called? Are you excited for this new system? Any big fears or concerns?

Thanks to everyone that took the time to participate. Don't forget to claim your $fxh!!!!

00:18 - Intro & Gorilla Sun (@gorillasu). Check out Gorilla's projects open for mint: Exotic Quarpets, Hex Appeal & Somewhere In Between

10:58 - Punevyr (@punevyr). Check out all of Punevyr's amazing history here on fx(hash)

15:29 - itsgalo (@GaloAndStuff). Check him out at galocanizares.com and please consider getting a PRINTERS book

27:42 - Pixel Symphony (@Pixel0Symphony). Give them a follow and learn more at pixelsymphony.art

38:41 - Rudxane (@rudxane1). See most of Rudxane's amazing body of work here on fx(hash) and here on Verse

41:43 - Julian Hespenheide (@julihespenheide). Learn more about Julian and his past works at julian-h.de

54:20 - David Seven (@D_VISION7). Learn more about David, his works, and his writings on his linktree

59:33 - Agoston Nagy (@_stc). Find links to Agoston's works and more at entangled.tools

01:10:46 - p1xelfool (@p1xelfool). See amazing renderings of his projects at p1xelfool.com

01:22:17 - Ty Vek (@ty__vek). ty-vek.art

01:25:58 Closing Remarks and Final Warnings

Episode Art by Julian Hespenheide

Intro and Outro tracks by Pixelwank

54:20 - David Seven (@D_VISION7). Learn more about David, his works, and his writings on his linktree

01:10:46 - p1xelfool (@p1xelfool). See amazing renderings of his projects at p1xelfool.com

item.title: Navigating The New $fxh Token Protocol: Interview with Ciphrd and Cosimo
item.description:
In this special episode of 'Waiting to Be Signed,' hosts Will & Trinity interview Ciphrd and Cosimo from the fx(hash) team to discuss the latest platform developments. They delve into the introduction of the $fxh token and its transformative potential for the generative art and NFT space. Key highlights include the evolution of the token protocol, its impacts on artists and collectors, and the innovative features of 'Open Form' projects, which allow continuous interaction with artworks. They also discuss the token's correlation with large edition artworks, its implications for the future of digital art, and how it aims to revitalize the art market amidst the ongoing bear market.

00:00 Introduction and Guest Welcome

01:15 Token Launch and Protocol Refinement

03:06 Open Form Art Mechanics

05:09 Evolutionary Art and Open Form

08:51 Tax and Legal Considerations

13:10 Why Base as the L2 for $fxh (and is Tezos cooked?)

19:05 $art Token Mechanics and Bonding Curves

28:52 Art and Finance Interplay

43:39 Primary Pricing, Ethereum Fee Structure, Legal Concerns

49:57 Future of Artist Tokens

54:20 FX Hash and Traditional Minting

57:20 Survival and Success of fx(hash)

01:11:00 Artist Participation and Market Dynamics

01:18:06 Conclusion and Final ThoughtsIntro & Outro music by Pixelwank

item.title: WTBS Cap *Live* on fx(hash)!!!
item.description:
Quick post here to let everyone know that the long-teased WTBS Cap is now up for sale on fx(hash).

https://www.fxhash.xyz/generative/slug/wtbs:-wearable-1-(cap-edition)

We partnered with Anna Lucia on this one to create a series of 1/1/x custom embroidered caps. She added a page to her website showing off all the designs here:

https://annalucia.io/wtbs-cap.html

Please read the project description for all the info on how to get yours + key commands to preview the hat designs. Please direct any questions you have to us on Twitter or Discord, DMs should be open.

We are so excited to finally have some form of merch for the show out there for you all to enjoy. Huge thanks && major gratitude to Anna Lucia and SimonSays for working with us to get this done.

item.title: Beautiful Utopias: Interview with Zancan
item.description:
In this posthumous episode of 'Waiting To Be Signed,' hosts Trinity & Will finally get their crack at interviewing Zancan, a pivotal figure in generative art and fx(hash). Zancan discusses his journey from a self-taught coder at age eight to becoming an engineer and eventually transitioning to a full-time artist. The episode covers his experiences with plotters, the transformative impact of blockchain and NFTs on generative art, and the development of Grass.js. Zancan reflects on his success with NFTs, the challenges of navigating the crypto art market, and the administrative tax hurdles that continue to plague him. The discussion also delves into personal growth, balancing professional and personal life, and the future of the NFT space.

01:59 Zancan's histort in art and coding

06:17 Becoming obsesses with plotters and how that led to creating generative algorithms

08:19 Finding the blockchain and fx(hash) as a means to release his work

09:42 The creation of Grass.js and the Impact of Garden, Monoliths on his career

19:45 Navigating success and challenges of crypto taxation

26:15 Struggling with feeling overwhelmed, burnout, and planning for the future

33:02 The evolution of Grass.js over time and the challenges of code optimization

39:38 The connection between digital and physical and becoming infatuated with embroidery

46:40 The freedom that comes with being uncommitted as an artist and find new opportunities to be creative

52:12 Hopes for the Future of NFTs

54:10 Gratitude and Final Thoughts

Episode Art: Monoliths #000 by Zancan (sold at auction for $111,265.95, December 2021

Intro & Outro tracks by Pixelwank

item.title: Getting Art Focused: Interview with NukCZ
item.description:
This episode we welcome collector, content creator and long-time listener NukCZ onto the show to talk to us about his journey into collecting generative art and his new Art Focused project. In this episode we discuss:

  • Nuk's journey from traditional collectibles to NFTs and generative art.
  • The origins of Nuk's 'walk and talk' series and connecting to his grandfather's art.
  • How Nuk creates the digital galleries that are toured in the Walk and Talks
  • Rodeo and other ways to explore and discover new artists
  • The future of generative art and the impact of technology on art collecting.
  • The community aspects enriching the NFT space and our personal experiences running WTBS.
  • Reflecting on 2.5 years of WTBS, our ongoing projects, and potential future plans.
  • Connecting physical goods to generative art as a way to expand the collector base

Thanks the Nuk for coming on the show! You can follow him on twitter @nukCZ_ and @artfocused_art

Check out Nuk's Art Focused youtube channel to catch up on his Walk and Talk series and artfocused.art for more of his writing and future projects.

Episode Art: Smolskull #311 by Mark Knol (owned by Nuk)

Intro & Outro tracks by Pixelwank

item.title: Holistic Internet Experience: Interview with Steve Pikelny (Stevie P)
item.description:
In this interview episode of Waiting to be Signed, hosts chat with artist Steve Pikelny, also known as Stevie P. They delve into his interactive web projects like FinSexy and his time as an Artblocks artist, balancing artistic expression with financialization. Steve also touches on the potential and perils of AI, the complexities of online dating, and his thoughts on the future of digital interactions in the art world. 00:00 Introduction and Guest Welcome 01:12 Steve's Journey to NFTs 02:27 Skepticism and Optimism in NFTs 08:13 Artistic Expression and User Manipulation 18:59 Balancing Art and Revenue 23:28 Interactive Projects and Community Engagement 27:28 Exploring Game Design 31:40 Exploring Alternative Platforms 33:09 The NFT Journey Begins 34:15 Success and Community in Artblocks 35:48 Shifting Focus and New Projects 38:18 Reflections on the Crypto Space 41:05 The Role of Financialization in Art 45:33 Medium and Themes in Art 49:38 Rapid Fire Questions

Follow Steve on Twitter @steviepxyz and learn more about his work at steviep.xyz

Follow us on twitter @waitingtosign and on Farcaster @wtbs

Episode art: Dopamine Machines #32 by Steve Pikelny

Intro & Outro tracks by PixelWank

item.title: On Generative Anesthetics: Interview with Kevin Esherick
item.description:
In this special episode of 'Waiting To Be Signed,' the hosts interview Kevin Esherick. They discuss his journey into coding and art, his innovative 'Material' jeans project, and his notable critique on generative art titled 'Generative Anesthetics.' Kevin also shares insights into the current and future landscape of art influenced by blockchain and AI, including his latest project to train an AI to autonomously produce art in his style.

00:00 Introduction and Guest Welcome

00:55 Kevin's Background and Journey into Coding

05:27 Kevin's Collaborative Jeans Project, Material and Introduction to NFTs

16:44 Generative Art Critique and Writing Process

23:13 Community Reactions to 'Generative Anesthetics' and Market Dynamics

37:14 Exploring the Spiritual and Abstract in Art

38:45 Balancing Retinal and Conceptual Art

39:43 Evaluating Art: Historical References and Originality

43:55 AI Art and the Concept of MangleCore

55:23 The Future of AI in Art and Society Follow Kevin on Twitter @kev_esh and learn more about his work at esherick.studio

Follow us on Twitter @waitingtosign and Farcaster @wtbs

Episode Art: Icarus by Kevin Esherick

Intro & Outro tracks by PixelWank

item.title: E120 - Guilt
item.description:
Courtesy of Descript:

In this reflective podcast episode, the hosts discuss the potential conclusion of their weekly show, considering an extended or possibly permanent hiatus. They explore the complexities of continuing the show amidst declining listenership and personal challenges. Conversations include updates on recent episodes, Trinity's return from a challenging work trip to Texas, and the frustrations of home repairs without interior doors. The hosts delve into the state of the generative art community, market trends, and their feelings about tackling diminishing energy and engagement. They also express their gratitude to supporters, announce potential merchandise, and contemplate future plans for the show's legacy. The episode concludes with personal reflections and addressing accountability, with both hosts expressing mutual understanding and appreciation.

00:00 Introduction and Episode Context 00:48 Trinity's Apartment and Work Trip 02:32 Upcoming Interviews and Show Hiatus 03:24 Reflections on Show's Journey and Challenges 05:42 Generative Art and Community Dynamics 22:08 Mental Health and Future Plans 29:24 Reflecting on Recent NFT Sales 30:16 Cool Drops and Art Projects 30:57 The Decline of Discord Conversations 32:42 Crypto Influencers and Politics 36:44 The Value of Crypto and Digital Ownership 39:48 Future Plans and Gratitude 42:01 Hearthstone and Personal Grievances 46:42 Final Reflections and Apologies Episode Art: Ode to GM #414 by artplusbrad

Intro & Outro tracks by PixelWank

Omitted segment music by Nor44

item.title: How To Start A Gallery On Another Continent: Interview with Mimi Nguyen
item.description:
WTBS adds another PhD to their esteemed list of guests! In this episode we discuss:

  • Mimi's journey into art and tech
  • How Mimi discovered NFT's and came to join Verse
  • Launching the Nguyen Wahed gallery
  • Mimi's thesis on on NFTs and the future of digital art
  • How the [aside] protocol works and how artists are using smart contracts to incorporate data into their work
  • How to curate and set up a group show
  • The challenges of displaying digital art
  • Opening a physical gallery in NYC and Mimi's hopes for their first show
  • Rapid Fire!

Thanks to Mimi for joining us, hope you all enjoyed this one.

Follow Mimi on Twitter @miminguyenmimi and her gallery @nguyenwahed

Learn more about Mimi and the Nguyen Wahed gallery at nguyenwahed.com

Follow us on Twitter @waitingtosign and on Farcaster @wtbs

item.title: Zoom In, Zoom Out: Interview with Travess Smalley
item.description:
Very excited to share out interview with Travess Smalley, famed for Pixel Rugs and just now releasing the incredible CRAWL on Feral File. Lots of notes for this one. We discuss:

  • How Travess got into art & coding, surf clubs, & NFTs
  • Pixel Rugs as a daily practice & the fandom surrounding them
  • CRAWL!!!! How does it work, what are the influences, and what was it like to work with Feral File on this release
  • Exploring organic textures in art & experimenting with printing techniques
  • Generative Art Conference in Berlin recap
  • Emergent play, creative software & being inspired by roguelike games
  • Rapid Fire (music, future guests & gaming with kids)

Travess also has a great set of links to things reference here that all should check out & enjoy

  • Worldmaking: A Guide To Minecraft Music
  • Bill Evans - From Left to Right
  • January Mornings
  • Travess' 2024 in review on Are.na
  • Games Research on Are.na
  • Creativity in Gameplay on Are.na
  • https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XlRVOziQQ7A

Games that influence CRAWL

  • Dungeon Crawl Stone Soup
  • Caves of Qud
  • Noita
  • Brogue
  • Dwarf Fortress
  • Sim Ant
  • Nethack
  • FTL: Faster Than Light

More Music!

  • Emahoy Tsegué-Maryam Guèbrou - Souvenirs
  • Patricia Escudero - Satie Sonneries
  • Noviciat Des Soeurs Missionnaires De Notre-Dame D’Afrique
  • Hans Joachim Roedelius
  • The Durutti Column
  • Dungeon Synth Archive on YouTube
  • Comfy Synth Archives on YouTube

Follow Travess on Twitter @travess

Follow us on Twitter @waitingtosign and on Farcaster @wtbs

If you like the show and want to support us you can subscribe to our Patreon or donate directly to wtbs.tez & wtbs.eth

item.title: E119 - Slop For The Piggies
item.description:
Alternate Title: The Piter Pasma of it All

In this episode of 'Waiting to be Signed,' hosts Trinity and Will discuss the week's events in the generative art world. They delve into recent market updates, notable platform news from Verse and FXHash, and examine Kevin Echerick's critical piece on generative art’s financialization. They also highlight new releases, successful art projects, and touch upon their personal experiences with the evolving dynamics of the digital art market. Concluding on a lighter note, the hosts share their recent adventures and strategies in Hearthstone.

  • What's Going on in Canada?
  • Verse Platform Updates
  • Known Origin Shutting Down && Prohibition is in trouble
  • Squiggle 9999 Donation to LACMA
  • Richard Kim's Embezzlement Confession && Coindesk coverage for balance
  • Generative Anesthetics by Kevin Esherick
  • Art Block Studio Projects and Challenges
  • The Space In Between by David Seven
  • Upcoming Projects
  • Hearthstone Battlegrounds Strategies and Observations

Thanks for listening! If you like the show and want to support us you can subscribe to our Patreon or donate directly to wtbs.tez & wtbs.eth

item.itunes:summary:
Alternate Title: The Piter Pasma of it All

In this episode of 'Waiting to be Signed,' hosts Trinity and Will discuss the week's events in the generative art world. They delve into recent market updates, notable platform news from Verse and FXHash, and examine Kevin Echerick's critical piece on generative art’s financialization. They also highlight new releases, successful art projects, and touch upon their personal experiences with the evolving dynamics of the digital art market. Concluding on a lighter note, the hosts share their recent adventures and strategies in Hearthstone.

  • What's Going on in Canada?
  • Verse Platform Updates
  • Known Origin Shutting Down && Prohibition is in trouble
  • Squiggle 9999 Donation to LACMA
  • Richard Kim's Embezzlement Confession && Coindesk coverage for balance
  • Generative Anesthetics by Kevin Esherick
  • Art Block Studio Projects and Challenges
  • The Space In Between by David Seven
  • Upcoming Projects
  • Hearthstone Battlegrounds Strategies and Observations

Thanks for listening! If you like the show and want to support us you can subscribe to our Patreon or donate directly to wtbs.tez & wtbs.eth

Follow us on Twitter @waitingtosign & Farcaster @wtbs

Episode Art: Re_01 by Marc Maurer

Intro & Outro tracks by PixelWank

item.title: Public Art & The Dance Between The Virtual and the Real: Interview with artist John Gerrard & Whitney Hart of Feral File
item.description:
In this episode we discuss:

  • John's history in art and simulation
  • Whitney's career in art and how she came to Feral File
  • The story of Western Flag becoming tokenized & John's decision to make NFTs
  • Reconciling the ecological cost of NFTs by supporting environmental initiatives & relationships with power
  • John's first tokenized work on the Bitmark chain
  • The two main issues that Whitney encounters when talking about NFTs to artists and collectors from the traditional world
  • The need for galleries and art fairs to catch up with the contemporary scene as it becomes increasingly digital
  • An overview of crystalline work (arctic) and the generative aspects of the performance
  • How Feral File worked with John to bring this experiment to life
  • Reconciling works that require patience against a population with a diminishing attention span
  • Time, data, the dating system for crystalline work and billionaires turning our consciousness into light
  • Newgrange
  • Blockchain as an alternative to oil-backed currencies with the potential to be a globally uniting force (...and pirates!)
  • The complicated dance between the unreal and the real

Follow John Gerrard on Twitter @john__gerrard and learn more about him at johngerrard.net

Follow Whitney Hart @h_artchronicles and follow Feral File @feralfile to keep up with their releases

Follow us on Twitter @waitingtosign, on Farcaster @wtbs and on Instagram @waitingtobesigned

If you like the show and want to support us you can subscribe to our Patreon or donate directly to wtbs.tez & wtbs.eth

Episode Art: crystalline work (arctic) 24.2.1.0 by John Gerrard

Intro & Outro tracks by PixelWank

item.title: E118 - VO2 MAX
item.description:
It's a post-holiday extravaganza! In this episode we discuss:

  • OBJKT launches it's generative art engine with the genesis project, 'uncertain index' by Pixelfool
  • A new anon Tezos whale loads up on a ton of great art
  • Tezos announces the Jstz L2 which will be JavaScript compatible
  • Tabor Robak & Verse announce the Broken Printer Recycling Program
  • Verse Solos will now function as an independent gallery on the platform
  • Verse is working on their own digital gallery app, Views
  • Art on Ledger Stax
  • Check us out on the GENART Podcast!
  • Discussion: What do you do when a collection stalls/doesn't sell out even after a long while??
  • Equinox by Aluan Wang on Verse
  • Biomechanika by Nekropunk on fx(hash)
  • Euneirophrenia by Liam Egan on fx(hash)
  • Yardoor by Asahamix on fx(hash)
  • fx(hash) announces their Base On-Chain Summer projects (looking ahead)
  • https://commit.unrulyunroll.xyz/

Thanks for listening as always. If you like the show and want to support us you can subscribe to our Patreon or donate directly to wtbs.tez & wtbs.eth

Episode Art: Equinox #2 by Aluan Wang

Intro & Outro tracks by PixelWank

item.title: Making Generative Art Less Random: Interview with James Merrill
item.description:
A special Monday interview episode for y'all! So pleased to finally record with James Merrill after meeting him a couple of times in person. Hope everyone enjoy this candid conversation about his art and the craziness of the web3 world.

In this episode we discuss:

  • James' background in art and coding, and how he got into NFTs
  • What James loves about Fidenza
  • Getting into plotter work and being included in Feral Files Graph+ exhibition in 2021
  • What is so cool about plotters anyways?
  • James' upcoming project, 'Busy'... and how does this even work with code?
  • 1A diversion into pens, inks, Schwittlick and KenConsumer
  • Art vs. Craft and (not) drawing a line in the sand for generative artists
  • Unionizing artists!
  • Some hot takes on AI art
  • Recent changes to Art Blocks and the potential for releasing on Studio vs other open platforms
  • Rapid Fire

Follow James on Twitter @toThePixel and find out more about him and his work here

Follow us on Twitter @waitingtosign, on Farcaster @wtbs and on Instagram @waitingtobesigned

If you like the show and want to support us you can subscribe to our Patreon and donate directly to wtbs.tez & wtbs.eth

Episode art: most of a 'Busy' by James Merril

Intro & Outro tracks by PixelWank

item.title: Entangled Cities: Interview with Bjørn Staal
item.description:
On the eve of on of the most exciting generative art releases of 2024, and the inaugural fx(hash) Vertex drop... we are so pleased to share with you our interview with Bjørn Staal aka _nonfigurativ_. Bjørn went viral last year when he shared his entangled particle systems interacting across two browser windows. His project is finally ready for release and coming to fx(hash) on both the Tezos and Ethereum blockchains.

In this episode we discuss:

  • Bjørn's background in art and coding
  • The story and history behind Entangled
  • Working with the fx(hash) team to be the first Vertex release
  • How Entangled works across chains & some behind-the-scenes tech talk
  • Minting dynamics and pricing a project for two chains & making a statement as an artist and platform
  • Bjørn's history with open-source and publishing the code behind Entangled (eventually)
  • The market, success, expectations & existing as a full-time artist in web3
  • Rapid fire!

Follow Bjørn on Twitter @_nonfigurativ_ and on Farcaster @nonfigurativ

Hear Bjørn on Arbitrarily Deterministic with Ken

Follow us on Twitter @waitingtosign, on Farcaster @wtbs and on Instagram @waitingtobesigned

If you like the show and want to support us you can subscribe to our Patreon or donate directly to wtbs.tez & wtbs.eth

Episode Art: Entangled by Bjørn Staal

Intro & Outro tracks by PixelWank

item.title: Magic Is Not Real: Interview with Erick Calderon (Round 2)
item.description:
Erick is back for Round 2 to discuss more about the market, web3 as a whole, the changes to AB and more!

In this episode we discuss:

  • Catching up on Erick's experiences since our last interview & skepticism of brand's with a web 3 strategy
  • The magic between an artist and their collector base & the metrics for success in the current ecosystem
  • Base chain, the power & sustainability of 'free' drops, the success of Pudgy Penguins
  • Onboarding collectors to generative art vs. web 3 as a whole
  • Addressing the current market with the new Curated and raising the bar with future releases to bring excitement back to collecting
  • Studio as a platform for artists to document all aspects of their work on-chain
  • The problem of preserving digital art long-term
  • What kind of generative goods could WTBS make as merchandise??

If you like the show and want to support us you can subscribe to our Patreon or donate directly to wtbs.tez and wtbs.eth

Episode Art: One More Day #41 by Aaron Penne

Intro & Outro tracks by PixelWank

item.title: Generative Art is a Website: Interview with Alejandro Campos
item.description:
Long time friend and artist Alejandro Campos joins WTBS for an interview ahead of his upcoming 'Fantasia' release on Verse. In this episode we discuss:

  • Alejandro's background in art and coding, how he got involved with NFTs & the origin of his original 'Ratchitect' artist name
  • Why Alejandro uses music in many of his projects & starting to discuss generative art through the lens of web design and experience.
  • Tackling problems and ideas that nobody else has before & making the p5.brush library + the work that goes into launching an open source project
  • Unearthing and reviving the defunct AI model that powers the music in Fantasia, Alejandro's upcoming Tender collab release on Verse
  • Where Fantasia sits in relation to the Enfantines series and Pensado a Mano
  • Popping off more about experimenting with interactivity, animation and sound in generative art + revisiting web development
  • Will there be more projects after Fantasia & disillusionment with web3
  • The story of Generative Architecture: The Making of a Room on Verse and the potential to curate more in the future
  • The weakness of 'digital' as a tool for preserving art
  • Rapid Fire!

Thanks to Alejandro for coming on the show! Be on the lookout for Fantasia coming to Verse in mid-June.

Follow Alejandro on Twitter to stay up to date on his work

Follow us on Twitter @waitingtosign, on Farcaster @wtbs and on Instagram @waitingtobesigned

If you like the show you can support us by subscribing to our Patreon and donating directly to wtbs.tez or wtbs.eth

Episode Art: Fantasia by Alejandro Campos

Intro & Outro tracks by PixelWank

item.title: E114 - Article Agreement
item.description:
E114 of WTBS is live! This week we discuss:

  • Trinity isn't sleeping, we had a huge week of recording & Will is going to Japan
  • A peak behind the curtain of our interview process
  • Is art made by code a gimmick?
  • What is 'excellence' and can you have excellent art without any context?
  • Lonliboy on originality in blockchain art and collectors finally becoming educated
  • and his follow-up or companion thread
  • On A New Aesthetics of Seriality by Holladay Saltz
  • Archetypes by Pixel Symphony (fxhash, base)
  • More discussion of Vacation by Roope & looking ahead to Alejandro's Fantasia as well

Thank you for listening, see you all when I am back from Japan!

If you like the show and want to support us you can subscribe to our Patreon or donate directly to wtbs.tez & wtbs.eth

item.title: The Distance of a Bird: Interview with Iskra Velitchkova
item.description:
WTBS is thrilled to have Iskra Velitchkova in the virtual studio for a long-overdue interview! In this episode we discuss:

  • Iskra's background in art, coding & data science and how she transitioned to creating art full time.
  • Finding inspiration to start working through books & "The Pause"
  • The connections of places to moments in Iskra's work & the transition to 'long form' generative art with Uninhabitable.
  • Will we ever recapture that feeling of HEN & early fx(hash) community?
  • Defining success and deciding between chains and platforms to release projects
  • The 'generative religion' of the NFT collector base & how collectors evaluate and define what they want to buy
  • The big question: What can we learn about ourselves through technology?
  • The potential to incorporate data into future projects
  • What might we see (or smell) from Iskra in the future?
  • The personal choice & struggle around committing to art as a career
  • A very brief Rapid Fire!

Thanks to Iskra for coming on the show and sharing so much with us. Follow her on Twitter @pointline_ to keep up with her work and learn more about her on her website iskraovelitchkova.com

Follow us on Twitter @waitingtosign, on Farcaster @wtbs and on Instagram @waitingtobesigned

If you like the show and want to support us you can subscribe to our Patreon or donate directly to wtbs.tez & wtbs.eth

Episode Art: Psychedelic chicken à la mode by Iskra

Intro & Outro tracks by PixelWank

  • The virtues of longboarding
  • g l y p h breaking the unofficial record for biggest genart project ever
  • Camille Roux starts a genart voicemail podcast!
  • Base announces OnChain Summer 2
  • Burning out on crypto, hating on the Fantasy Top shilling & all the new tipping meme coins
  • OpenSea stats are not looking good
  • Tack Line Torn on new platform Intrinsic.Art
  • Displaced by Rob Scalera
  • Civic Nostalgia by 0xBenji
  • Allegro by PixelWank
  • Collection by Serezha Galkin
  • Strange Loops by Mark Webster
  • The dual-chain particle project from nonfigurativ looks to finally be coming to fx(hash) + other Looking Ahead

Thanks for listening as always! If you like the show and want to support us you can subscribe to our Patreon or donate directly to wtbs.eth or wtbs.tez

item.title: Uncompromising Collaborations: Interview with Rich Poole & Rick Crane
item.description:
Rich & Rick, two legendary fx(hash) collaborators, finally get the WTBS treatment! In this episode we discuss:

  • Their backgrounds as artists and the origin of their collaborative practice
  • The story behind 'Miniscapes', their first collab project on fx(hash)
  • Their collaborative process and how Rich brings the "generative-ness" to Rick's design & aesthetic + the challenges of adaptation.
  • The story of Acequia and breaking through to a wider set of collectors outside of fx(hash)
  • The process behind IsoMetro, their upcoming release, and how they came to release with 8NAP
  • Will there be cars in IsoMetro & how it relates to Acequia
  • How do Rich & Rick think about the market now, picking platforms and chains for release
  • Rich "accidentally" donating his only Acequia to WTBS
  • Rapid Fire!

Thanks to Rich & Rick for coming on the show! Check out their upcoming relase, IsoMetro, on 8NAP Art

Follow us on Farcaster @wtbs, Twitter @waitingtosign & Instagram @waitingtobesigned

If you like the show you can support us by subscribing to our Patreon & donating directly to wtbs.tez and wtbs.eth

Episode Art: IsoMetro WIP courtesy of Rich & Rick

Intro & Outro tracks by PixelWank

item.title: Computational Creativity: Interview with Erik Swahn
item.description:
Join us as we welcome fx(hash) & overall genart legend Erik Swahn to his very first podcast appearance! We have been fans and collectors of Erik for 2+ years now and are thrilled to have him on the show to discuss his practice & upcoming release, Autoscope, on Verse

In this episode we discuss:

  • Erik's background in art & coding and how he discovered NFTs as a way to release his work
  • Erik's earliest fx(hash) releases, Retrogrades and Disegnatori, and how they play into the overall themes of his work
  • Farbteiler's origin as a synesthetic system for learning piano + palettes and color construction
  • Punktwelt, Fields & Erik's current style as it has evolved from Farbteiler
  • Embracing 'Collector Curated' as a mechanism for release & collectors hitting the mark in their selections
  • Maintaining a consistent visual style without fatiguing collectors
  • Autoscope on Verse & adding animation to create a sense of place to the work
  • Releasing with Verse for Erik's last 3 projects and how he thinks about platforms and blockchains
  • Rapid Fire!

Thanks to Erik for coming on the show! Follow him on Twitter @erikswahn and on Farcaster @erikswahn to keep up with his work.

Follow us on Farcaster @wtbs, on Twitter @waitingtosign and on Instagram @waitingtobesigned

If you like the show you can support us by subscribing to our Patreon and donating directly to wtbs.tez & wtbs.eth

Episode Art: Autoscope seed by Erik Swahn, TBD if it will be minted :)

Intro & Outro tracks by PixelWank

item.title: Evolving Verse: Interview with Jamie Gourlay, Founder of Verse
item.description:
We welcome Jamie for a return interview, a little over one year later since we last had him on! In this episode we discuss:

  • Big lessons and takeaways for Jamie & Verse coming out of the 2023 bear market
  • Consolidation of artists towards curated platforms & competing for releases
  • Verse as a conduit for the gallery art world to access web3 and what exciting release they have coming soon as a test case
  • Managing web3 communities as an artist and the need to be connected & authentic
  • Verse becoming more web3 oriented with recent announcements
  • The role of the market in onboarding new collectors & how new artists can break through without using open platforms
  • Verse collecting & supporting artists that release with them
  • Solana & the potential for Verse to add other chains moving forward
  • Reacting to the recent announcement from Art Blocks & cross-platform collaborations
  • The goal of a potential Verse community token

Thanks to Jamie! Follow Verse on Twitter and join their Discord for updates

Follow us on Twitter @waitingtosign, Farcaster @wtbs and Instagram @waitingtobesigned

If you want to support the show you can subscribe to our Patreon & donate directly to wtbs.tez and wtbs.eth

Episode Art: Funktor #125 by Erik Swahn

Intro & Outro tracks by PixelWank

item.title: Building The Next Wave of GenArt Lovers: Interview with Erick Calderon
item.description:
Kicking off a series of interviews with platform founders, we are thrilled to bring you this conversation with Erick Calderon aka Snowfro. We dive deep into the recently announced changes with AB, Sansa, and the market as a whole.

In this episode we discuss:

  • Racing cars, Magic: the Gathering & Minecraft
  • Surviving in Crypto for 7+ years, surviving the 2018 bear market and our current downswing & the simple math behind the generative art market
  • What the experience could be for new collectors that are no longer "early"
  • Why buy Sansa? What do they bring to Art Blocks and their role in the future of the platform?
  • Who will be allowed to release on Sansa & the challenges of being a truly open platform
  • Expanding Engine Partners to brands & bringing new communities to web3 through physical generative objects
  • How Curated is changing and when we can expect the next Curated release
  • Can we expect new chains, sale mechanisms, release styles & more from Art Blocks & Sansa?
  • A brief Marfa pitch

Follow Erick on Twitter @ArtOnBlockchain and join the Art Blocks Discord to stay up to date on their upcoming announcements

If you like the show you can support us by subscribing to our Patreon or donating directly to wtbs.tez & wtbs.eth

Follow us on Farcaster @wtbs, Twitter @waitingtosign & Instagram @waitingtobesigned

Episode Art: Chromie Squiggle #7 by Snowfro

Intro and Outro tracks by PixelWank

item.title: What Is Etherlink?: Interview with Nicolas Streschinsky
item.description:
Nicolas Streschinsky, head of DeFi at Trillitech, joins us to talk about the upcoming launch of Etherlink. Thanks to Nicolas and the Trillitech team for helping to arrange this episode. We discuss:

  • Who is Nicolas and how did he get into Crypto?
  • What are the goals of Trillitech and why did they start the Etherlink project?
  • What makes Etherlink different from other L2s?
  • How will users interact with Etherlink & the potential of ERC-4337 to scale and onboard new users
  • What is the customer acquisition model for Etherlink?
  • Product-Market fit and the problem with Airdrops & Points
  • How bridging between Tezos, Etherlink & other EVM L2s will work with Layer Zero

Check out Nicolas talking about Etherlink at Next Block Expo and join the Etherlink Discord to learn more about the project

Follow us on Farcaster @wtbs, Twitter @waitingtosign and Instagram @waitingtobesigned

If you like the show you can support us by subscribing to our Patreon and donating directly to wtbs.tez & wtbs.eth

Episode Art: The Etherlink Logo!

Intro & Outro tracks by PixelWank

This week everyone is sick, or caring for a sick baby, or both... so thank you in advance for understanding! This episode we discuss:

  • Thanks to everyone who helped us get our 'Active User' badge on Farcaster!
  • TASCHEN releases an NFT art book
  • The Finnish National Gallery is holding a generative art competition and you can enter!
  • fx(hash) splits the explore page to filter out image comp projects
  • Zancan breaks 60k mints with Aux Arbres & new traits continue to emerge

item.title: Baudrillard, Blockchains & Barbarians: Interview with Jacek Markusiewicz
item.description:
WTBS lands a dream guest, Jacek Markusiewicz! Both of us have been following and collecting work by Jacek since his earliest days releasing on fx(hash). We are so excited to share this episode with him ahead of his Verse Solos release, Barbarians! In this episode we discuss:

  • Jacek's background in art and coding & how he came to Tezos to start releasing his work as NFTs
  • Architectural history and the story behind Unbuilt and Reborn, two of his earliest works on fx(hash)
  • The concept behind Hollow & the experience of 'breaking through' with a release in such a big way
  • The story of Barbarians, how it relates to his more recent work & the duality of existence!
  • The pressure of curating and how Barbarians will be released on Verse
  • Strategy for releasing work, picking platforms & thinking about newer chains that are emerging as places for art
  • rapid fire!

Follow Jacek on Twitter @jMarkusiewicz and explore his work here

Follow us on Twitter @waitingtosign & Farcaster @wtbs & instagram @waitingtobesigned

If you like the show you can support us by subscribing to our Patreon or donating directly to wtbs.tez & wtbs.eth

Episode Art: Barbarians by Jacek Markusiewicz

item.title: E105 - *Checks Notes*
item.description:
E105 is live and xtra long!

In this episode we discuss:

  • Baby stuff & solo parenting
  • Miss Metaverse & the pain, drain & necessity of using social media as a creative
  • Trinity sniped me with a birthday tweet
  • Yuga buys Moonbirds & not everyone is bullish on it
  • fx(hash) is adding the Base Chain L2 on Feb 28 (and what does this mean for Tezos?)

item.description:
New week new art new show. In this episode we discuss:

  • Will ran a game of Survivor and crushed it!
  • Verse onboards over 100 new users with their move to Solana
  • Follow Trinity on Twitter to see her #TENDERED posts

item.title: Structural Complexity Through Visual Clarity: Interview with Kjetil Golid
item.description:
The interview streak continues! This week we are excited to talk to Kjetil Golid ahead of his Monument release on Verse. In this episode we discuss:

  • Kjetil's background in art and coding & how he first discovered the blockchain.
  • The history behind Archetype and Paper Armada, how they came to Art Blocks and released so close together (plus how one collector traded a Fidenza for an Armada!)
  • Creating systems and whether the art is the code or the output
  • Kjetil's use of color and evolving palettes over time
  • The story behind Monument, the influence of brutalist design in Kjetil's work, and returning to long form
  • The change in royalties on Ethereum and how Kjetil thinks about chains and platforms for releasing projects\
  • Rapid fire!

Thanks to Kjetil for coming on the show. Please check out his website generated.space for more info on his work

If you like the show and want to support us you can subscribe to our Patreon or donate directly to wtbs.tez & wtbs.eth

Episode Art: Monument by Kjetil Golid

Intro & Outro tracks by PixelWank

item.title: E103 - Gold Star
item.description:
This week on WTBS we are talking art, kissing boys, and wondering about Solana! In this episode we discuss:

  • Interview roundup! Be sure to check out our interview with Jan Robert Leegte + we have Kjetil Golid and Jacek Markusiewicz coming up!
  • Sharing art on social media, the #TENDERED initiative, and helping to promote newer projects
  • Newer Wave by bleaker.art is available to mint on fxhash
  • iRyanBell is back on fxhash with Can't Stop, Won't Stop, an open edition on ETH that is available until Feb 14.
  • Interactive generative pottery is also back via fxVase by KilledByAPixel
  • Check out Glassy Love by Erfan Hosseini over on Highlight and see if you can catch a low-gas moment to mint
  • The Truth series by ClownVamp concludes with it's last chapters
  • Ridge Regressions by Pierre Casadebeig x Tender
  • Heuristics of Emotion by Jeres drops on Solana and spawns a lot of discussion between the hosts.

Thanks as always for listening. If you like the show and want to support us you can subscribe to our Patreon or donate directly to wtbs.tez & wtbs.eth

Follow us on twitter @waitingtosign on Farcaster @wtbs and on Instagram @waitingtobesigned

item.title: Sculpting The Web: Interview with Jan Robert Leegte
item.description:
We are thrilled to bring you this special interview episode with net art legend Jan Robert Leegte! In this episode we discuss:

  • Jan's background in art & coding + the story of how Harm van den Dorpel convinced him to give NFTs a shot
  • The internet as a public good and the philosophy of applying ownership to it with blockchain
  • How the culture of the internet has changed
  • Bringing physicality of digital things
  • Mountains and Dropshadows on Verse, why Jan re-visited the concept and his experience using AI
  • The story behind Web and AI's insufficient creativity at coding interesting websites
  • How Jan thinks about markets, blockchains & the preservation of his work

Big thanks of course to Jan for coming on the show! Follow Jan on Twitter @JanRobertLeegte to keep up with his releases and check out his website full of his past work

If you like the show you can support us by subscribing to our Patreon or donating directly to wtbs.tez & wtbs.eth

Episode Art: Dmuceton from Web by Jan Robert Leegte

Intro & Outro tracks by PixelWank

item.title: Feeling Infinity: Interview with LoVid (Tali Hinkis & Kyle Lapidus)
item.description:
NYC duo LoVid are in the studio and sharing a mic for a very special WTBS interview. Please check out their upcoming release on Tonic, Heartsleeves, which we discuss a bit in the episode.

In this episode we discuss:

  • The story of how Tali & Kyle met and formed LoVid
  • The secret to successfully collaborating for 20+ years
  • How LoVid work with code collaborators to bring an analogue feel to their NFT releases
  • The story of Heartsleeves, the previous projects that influence it, and how it came to be released with Tonic
  • The role of identity and anonymity in web3 and how it plays into the interactive side of Heartsleeves
  • Textiles & weaving and the role they play in LoVid's work
  • Advice for artists thinking about entering web3... or trying to cross over from web3 to the 'traditional' art world
  • Kids, noise music, and other rapid fire questions
  • Some music links from Tali: A Cause Des Garcons by Yelle & Shitloads of Money by Liz Phair

Follow LoVid on Twitter @lovidlovid and learn more about them at their linktree

Follow us on Twitter @waitingtosign and Instagram @waitingtobesigned && farecaster @wtbs

If you like the show and want to support us you can subscribe to our Patreon or donate direct to wtbs.tez or wtbs.eth

Episode Art is taken from the upcoming Heartsleeves release

Intro & Outro tracks by PixelWank

item.description:

  • Highlight also releases an ecosystem report on the trends with royalties across NFTs

Short and sweet this week

item.title: Minimum Viable Mountain: Interview with Pierre Casadebeig
item.description:
Welcome to another interview episode! We are thrilled to be chatting with Pierre Casadebeig ahead of his long-awaited Tender collaboration: Ridge Regressions. In this episode we discuss:

  • Who is Pierre & how did he get into making art?
  • Using R to make art and Pierre's attempt at working with p5
  • How Pierre uses data in his art and where the generative-ness comes from in this approach
  • The significance of mountains to Pierre and the role of accuracy with his work
  • The story of the Tender collaboration and why it took so long :)
  • How Ridge Regressions became the collab and why it incorporates new visual styles and calligraphy
  • Other date-rich areas that Pierre might explore with his work
  • Rapid Fire questions & science talk

Thanks to Pierre and to everyone who listened

Follow Pierre on Twitter @pircasa and follow us @waitingtosign

If you like the show you can support us by subscribing to our Patreon or donating directly to wtbs.tez or wtbs.eth

Episode Art: Ridge Regressions #8 by Pierre Casadebeig

item.title: Perfection Isn't Interesting: Interview with Frank Manzano & Artie Handz
item.description:
WTBS is back with our first interview of 2024, a special episode with artist Frank Manzano & collector/curator Artie Handz! The Artie Gallery is bringing "ScheduledProgramming" to Verse on January 11, 2024. In this episode we discuss:

  • Artie's background in art & collecting and how he came to start his Gallery with Verse
  • Frank's background in art & AI and how he got started selling his work as NFTs
  • How Artie first found Frank's work and why they are working together for this first Gallery release
  • The dystopic themes in Frank's work and finding hope in unsettling subjects
  • The excitement of discovery with AI, Frank's process, and what A.I. brings to the work that couldn't be found elsewhere
  • Legal issues arriving with A.I. and how Artie evaluates A.I. work as a collector
  • Rapid Fire

Follow Artie Handz on Twitter @punk7635

Follow Frank Manzano on Twitter @loved_orleer

Follow us on Twitter @waitingtosign and on Instagram @waitingtobesigned

If you like the show and want to support us you can subscribe to our Patreon or donate directly to wtbs.tez & wtbs.eth

Intro and Outro tracks by Pixelwank

  • How Artie first found Frank's work and why they are working together for this first Gallery release
  • The dystopic themes in Frank's work and finding hope in unsettling subjects
  • The excitement of discovery with AI, Frank's process, and what A.I. brings to the work that couldn't be found elsewhere
  • Legal issues arriving with A.I. and how Artie evaluates A.I. work as a collector
  • Rapid Fire

Follow Artie Handz on Twitter @punk7635

Follow Frank Manzano on Twitter @loved_orleer

Follow us on Twitter @waitingtosign and on Instagram @waitingtobesigned

If you like the show and want to support us you can subscribe to our Patreon or donate directly to wtbs.tez & wtbs.eth

Intro and Outro tracks by Pixelwank

item.title: E97 - The 2023 Mega Episode
item.description:
As we enter Year 3 of the show, our 100th episode, and probably some other milestones we are forgetting... we close out 2023 with a mega year-in-review episode. We go over the major themes of the year, our favorite projects, and attempt a few predictions for 2024. Some brief notes:

  • Will got his Generations quilt :)
  • A pensive first discussion of Solana
  • Galaxy is running out of RGB's to sell & the Index
  • Going over the big themes of 2023, what we got right and what we missed
  • Reviewing our favorite projects of the year!
  • 2024 predictions!

If you like the show you can support us by subscribing to our Patreon or donating directly to wtbs.tez or wtbs.eth

item.title: The Cult of Generative Art: Interview w/ Bre Pettis
item.description:
Bre Pettis is a generative art collector, plotter enthusiast, puppeteer and co-founder of MakerBot. We got a hint of Bre's background when he introduced himself in Tender discord some months back and got connected to bring you this collector interview episode. This one takes a few surprising turn, we hope you enjoy! In this episode we discuss:

  • Bre's background in art & collecting
  • Bre's current company Bantam and his role in the upcoming Harold Cohen exhibition at the Whitney in NYC
  • How Bre got into NFTs
  • fx(hash) 2.0 and the Ethereum integration + how Bre feels about Eth vs Tez
  • Artist/Collector Curated vs. Long Form
  • How Bre views his own collecting and his plan to sell
  • Hunting for plottable art on fx(hash) & shouting out some of Bre's favorites
  • Hopes for 2024 in generative art
  • Bre tells us a Magic: the Gathering story :)

Big thanks to Bre for taking the time to come on the show and chat with us!

If you enjoy WTBS you can support us by subscribing to our Patreon or donate direct to wtbs.tez or wtbs.eth

Follow us on Twitter @waitingtosign & Instagram @waitingtobesigned

Episode Art: Yip & Yap's Imaginary Playground #36 by Piter Pasma

Intro & Outro tracks by Pixelwank

item.title: E94 - Thirteenth Hour
item.description:
E94 of WTBS, recorded 12/7/23 and released 12/11/23.

In this episode we discuss:

  • News: fx(hash) 2.0 ETH integration delayed, Art Basel Miami & Vera Molnar passes
  • Topic: Reacting to the 2.0 delay, start-up mentality, what the fx(hash) team did right and looking forward to the proper launch on 12/14
  • Will buys a grey Smollskull & HAL09999 is a favorite of the week with The WRK Paradox
  • Tender & Verse crushing the Untitled fair at Art Basel Miami with the Dimensionality Exhibition, featuring work by quibibi, Zach Lieberman, Anna Lucia, Licia He, Cory Haber, Marcel Schwittlick, Emily Edelman & Aranda\Lasch
  • Tezos projects still dropping on fx(hash) including Smollskulls x Monster Gaze, The Soul of Flowers & 5-Grid Compression
  • Lots of mintable projects to shout out!
  • Bonus Magic: The Gathering discussion :)

Thanks for listening as always! If you want to support the show you can subscribe to our Patreon or donate directly to wtbs.tez & wtbs.eth

Follow us on Twitter @waitingtosign and Instagram @waitingtobesigned

item.title: The Aesthetic of a Pixel: Interview with Kim Asendorf
item.description:
We are very excited to complete the trifecta by bringing you a classic interview episode with Kim Asendorf. We are sharing this just a day before the AGH1 exhibition at Funkhaus in Berlin, where Kim is presenting work alongside Leander Herzog and Andreas Gysin. In this episode we discuss:

  • Kim's background in art and coding & the journey to selling work as NFTs
  • The appeal of pixels and what exactly is 'Pixel Sorting'?
  • The story of Reading A Book and how it ended up on fx(hash)
  • The jump to long-form after the original Monogrids series
  • Pushing boundaries with art & innovation vs. what collector's might find beautiful
  • The meaninglessness of being 'first' in blockchain & hopes for future appreciation of challenging artwork
  • Finding the narrative of expressive artwork and naming works
  • What early game & computer moments still influence Kim's work
  • The story of the upcoming AGH1 exhibition
  • How Kim thinks about platform & chain for releasing work in the current NFT ecosystem

If you like what we are doing and want to support the show you can subscribe to out Patreon or donate directly via wtbs.tez or wtbs.eth

Follow us on Twitter @waitingtosign or Instagram @waitingtobesigned

Episode Art by Kim Asendorf

Intro & Outro tracks by Pixelwank

item.title: WTBS x Kaloh fx(hash) Collector's Guide
item.description:
With the holiday in the US last week + the slow down as we wait for 2.0, we didn't do a normal episode... instead we went on Kaloh's Podcast to talk about about all the amazing & undervalued projects on fx(hash) for new collectors to check out. Here are links to everything we talk about:

  • Zancan - Garden Monoliths, (kinder)Garden Monuments, A Bugged Forest
  • William Mapan - Dragons
  • Ciphrd - RGB, Ethereal Microcosm
  • Kim Asendorf - Reading a Book, tr4ns4ctions, NAZCA, Zoom
  • Msoriaro - Contrapuntos
  • Lars Wander - Gossamer, Unfolded, Geode
  • Iskra Velitchkova & Zach Lieberman - Uninhabitable, Horizon(te)s
  • Yazid - Hashed Arcs, Hashed Cities, Step By Step
  • Melissa Wiederrecht - Marble Opulence, Orbs, Take Wing, Zbaggeti
  • Ivona Tau - Study of Atom, Mythic Latent Glitches
  • Thomas Noya - VHX-1, FHX-1, The400Flips, Ciudad Central, a fortiori, CHX-1
  • Jeres - Coronado
  • Casey Reas - The Century-XXX Series
  • Mark Knol - Smolskull
  • Andreas Gysin - Towers, Device 1, Smooth Steps
  • Cyril Diagne - Waxing Crescent & check out Pixelfiller
  • Anna Lucia - Art For Walls In Public Spaces
  • Rudxane - Bingo, Tych

If you like the show and want to support us you can subscribe to our Patreon or donate directly to wtbs.tez or wtbs.eth

item.title: Your Ultimate Guide to fx(hash) 2.0 w/ Ciphrd & Adam Berninger
item.description:
Will & Trinity are excited to bring you the XXL definitive launch day guide to fx(hash) 2.0! We are joined by Ciphrd & Adam Berninger (ajberni of Tender) to talk all about the platform update, art to look forward to, and plenty more. In this episode we discuss:

  • A quick rundown of 2.0 features + the potential for fiat pricing in 2024
  • Tender as a launch week partner, day 1 releases, and what artists are excited about with 2.0
  • How the addition of Ethereum might impact pricing and markets for Tezos projects
  • ONCHFS: How to pronounce it and how the new on-chain system works
  • The shift in fx(hash) towards partnership and increased discoverability via the new release calendar
  • The pre-rendered image poll results & the future of artist curated work on fx(hash)
  • Looking back at 2023 and surviving a year in the bear market
  • How fx(hash) can encourage users to trade on the platform and socially enforce royalties for Ethereum projects
  • The future of Tender in 2024
  • Will Ciphrd have a release for 2.0 launch???

Follow us on Twitter @waitingtosign and Instagram @waitingtobesigned

If you like the show and wants to support us you can subscribe to our Patreon or donate direct to wtbs.tez and wtbs.eth

Episode Art: Untitled WIP from Ciphrd :)

Intro and Outro tracks by Pixelwank

item.title: Waiting To Sit: Interview with Luke Shannon
item.description:
Will & Trinity sit down with Luke Shannon to discuss everything about chairs, chair-ness, and his his upcoming 'Seating Arrangements' release on Tonic.xyz

In this episode we discuss:

  • Luke's background in art & coding, and how he introduced generative processes to his practice
  • How algorithmic exploration unlocks new understanding of a medium
  • Luke's inspiration for working on a generative chair & some of the technical aspects to describing a chair through code
  • The value of friction and discomfort in designed objects
  • The tie between physical and digital and the role NFTs play in Luke's work
  • The longevity of blockchains and preservation

Sorry for Will's technical issues on this one :)

Big thanks to Luke for coming on the show and talking to us for so long about chairs! You can follow Luke on Twitter @luke_shannon_ and check out his website to learn more about his work.

Follow us on Twitter @waitingtosign and Instagram @waitingtobesigned

Support the show by subscribing on Patreon or donating directly to wtbs.tez or wtbs.eth

Episode Art: Seating Arrangements by Luke Shannon

  • The Pre Auction Extravaganza episode of Nota Bene
  • SBF Guilty and a reminder for anyone interest to check out coverage of the trial on the Unchained Podcast

  • fx(hash) hype season officially starts with 2.0 launch date announced for December 1 and artists scheduling releases on the new Calendar feature
  • Trinity gets a Tych on collection offer, one of many pieces sold by Galaxy in this latest round of sales.
  • Cosmic Poems by Mark Webster on fx(hash)
  • Trichro-matic by MountVitruvius on Art Blocks Curated
  • Constraint by Eric Andwer on Verse
  • In Conversation With live at Paris Photo and for sale on Verse
  • Catching up on a lot of shoutouts

Thanks for listening as always!

If you enjoy the show and want to support us, you can subscribe to our Patreon or donate directly to wtbs.tez or wtbs.eth

Intro and Outro music by Pixelwank

item.title: Cursor Lines & Drawing Machines: Interview with Marcel Schwittlick
item.description:
Marcel Schwittlick is a Berlin-based artist best known for his work with drawing machines. We have been fans of Marcel's work since we first discovered his Upward Spiral series and have been following from platform to platform since then.

In this interview we discuss:

  • Marcel's background in art and coding & how he discovered NFTs
  • The "generative-ness" of his work with drawing machines & building entropy with cursor lines
  • Honoring pens and ink with his The Long Run series
  • How the Octet performance came about & using A.I. to network 8 different plotting machines
  • The influence of the Concrete art movement on Marcel's work with Luminograms & his upcoming exhibition of Composition 92 at Paris Photo (and here is a link to the Verse page for his works & others)
  • Marcel's thoughts on Tezos and Ethereum, and whether he would replace a lost or destroyed physical piece for a collector

Thanks to Marcel for taking the time to speak with us for the show. You can learn more about him and his work on his website, schwittlick.net and you can follow him on Twitter @schwittlick_

You can follow us on Twitter @waitingtosign and on Instagram @waitingtobesigned

If you like the show you can support us by subscribing to our Patreon or donating directly to wtbs.tez or wtbs.eth

Episode Art: Composition #92 (Luminogram) #3 by Marcel Schwittlick

Intro & Outro music by Pixelwank

item.title: E90 - The Rumor Mill
item.description:
Big week for "breaking" news on the show.

E90 was recorded 10/27/23 and released 10/30/23. In this episode we discuss:

  • Trinity visits Artmatr in Brooklyn to check out the the Reflections exhibition, curated by Tender's very own ajberni.
  • fx(hash) announces their fx(hackathon) winners, 10 projects using fx(params) winning 1,000tz each!
  • Rumors & Speculation: we discuss Artblocks moving to a 30% platform fee in 2024. What does this mean for AB and the the field of generative art platforms?
  • Sketchbook A by William Mapan mints out near $11k a pass!
  • the reGen project series on Art Blocks release in support of Alzheimer's research

Another short-ish episode, we hope you all enjoy!

You can follow us on Twitter @waitingtosign or Instagram @waitingtobesigned.

Support the show by subscribing to our Patreon. Patrons that donate $25 or more a month will be shouted out at the end of each episode!

You can also donate direct to wtbs.tez or wtbs.eth.

Episode Art: Sketchbook A #24 by William Mapan (minted by unclepennybags.eth)

item.title: Physicals in a Digital Art World: Interview with Susannah Maybank, co-founder of Tonic.xyz
item.description:
Susannah Maybank is the co-founder of Tonic.xyz, a generative art platform that is built around the production of high quality prints for each of their releases. Susannah crossed over from the legacy art world and has a passion for educating collectors about generative art & NFTs.

In this episode we discuss:

  • Susannah's background and her career in the legacy art world
  • The story behind the founding of Tonic and the early days of the company
  • How Tonic works to cross over new collectors to the space & what the major challenges are in educating collectors around NFTs
  • The challenges & process of coming up with novel print options for Tonic and what kind of engagement they are seeing on these offerings
  • How Tonic identifies & pitches artists that they want to work with and what that process is like from start to release
  • Will NFT art markets start to become more like traditional markets?
  • The tension between market forces and algorithmic potential, and why it can be good to constrain the number of outputs for a release
  • Excitement for a digitally native art movement & the need for more criticism and conversation around NFTs.

Thanks to Susannah for taking the time to come on the show!

Follow Susannah on Twitter @SusannahMaybank and follow Tonic on Twitter @Tonic_xyz to keep up with their future releases!

Follow us on Twitter @waitingtosign and Instagram @waitingtobesigned

You can support the show by subscribing to our Patreon or donating directly to wtbs.tez or wtbs.eth

Episode Art: Rhythm & The Machine #0 by Lars Wander

Intro and Outro music by Pixelwank

item.title: Translating Hand to Code: Interview with William Mapan
item.description:
WTBS is pleased to bring you another exciting interview episode. We were fortunate to record with William a few weeks before his Verse Solos release, Intimacy. Unfortunately Trinity could not make the recording session for this one :(

In this episode we discuss:

  • William's background in art and coding & how he found the blockchain
  • The early days of Tezos & fx(hash) and how Dragons became one of the 'grail' projects of the platform.
  • Anticyclone and the lessons learned from Dragons that made for his successful release on Art Blocks Curated.
  • William's philosophy around trat distribution, rarity & the floor pieces of Distance, his most recent release with LACMA
  • Sketchbook A & the process for writing an algorithm to mimic a physical medium
  • How Sketchbook A will be released, going small, and making the leap to 'collector curated'
  • How Through Your Eyes relates and contrasts with Sketchbook A
  • Williams paintings, future plans for the Sketchbook series, and of course... rapid fire :)

Thanks to William for taking the time to come on the show and speak to us for this interview!

You can follow William on Twitter @williamapan and check out his linktree for more information about him and his work

You can follow us on Twitter @waitingtosign and Instagram @waitingtobesigned

If you would like to support the show you can subscribe to our Patreon or donate directly to wtbs.tez and wtbs.eth

Episode Art: Through Your Eyes #2 by William Mapan

Intro and Outro music by Pixelwank

item.title: Building The Generative Bazaar: Interview with Nat Emodi, founder of Highlight.xyz
item.description:
In this special interview episode we welcome Nat Emodi, founder and CEO of Highlight.xyz. We learned about highlight recently for their participation in the On-Chain Summer event hosted on the Base chain. Thanks to Nat for coming on and sharing his vision for Highlight and the future of generative art!

In this episode we discuss:

  • How Nat got into crypto & generative art and the story of the founding of Highlight
  • Why another platform? What makes Highlight different and what are the benefits (and risks) of giving artists the tools to own their own contract
  • Ethereum, ETH L2s, and the UX challenges of the current crypto experience.
  • Highlight's economic model & betting on the potential of generative art
  • Highlight's role in homepage curation & collaborating with artists
  • Royalties!

Follow Nat @natemodi and Highlight @highlight_xyz

Follow us @waitingtosign and consider donating to wtbs.tez or wtbs.eth

Episode art is 1,000 True Fans from Leander Herzog's on-chain summer drop!

Intro and Outro music by PixelWank

item.title: Helping Artists Achieve Their Goals: Interview with SimonSays
item.description:
SimonSays is a generative art collector and appreciator that recently converted his love for Art into a career as an artist manager & agent. In this interview we dive into Simon's background in art and collecting, how he linked up with the artists he represents, his mission for enabling artists to achieve their goals, and a whole lot of market talk! Huge thanks to SimonSays for taking the time to come on the show and speak candidly about his business.

Follow Simon on Twitter @0xSimonSays or find him in one of the many art-related Discords like Tender or Grailers.

Check out the artists that work with Simon: Marcel Schwittlick, Zach Lieberman, & Efdot

Follow us on Twitter @waitingtosign and on Instagram @waitingtobesigned

item.title: There Is No Offline: Interview with Maya Man
item.description:
WTBS welcomes Maya Man to the show for a fantastic interview episode. Maya has an amazing Verse Solo's exhibition releasing this week, "I'm Feeling Lucky" that explores the relationship between astrology, numerology, and The Internet. Please go check it out! In this episode we discuss:

  • A brief into of how Maya got into making art with code & releasing projects as NFTs
  • Getting exposed to astrology in LA and the online structures of identity
  • The logistics of building "I'm Feeling Lucky" and how it relates to "Fake It 'Till You Make It"
  • The personal aspect of "Read It And Weep"
  • Cyborgs and feminism
  • Growing up online, the fear + shame of posting, and the performance of 'self' on the internet
  • How astrology and generative art overlap
  • Finding comfort in simulation theory and determinism
  • Humor in art and the internet
  • Rapid Fire Questions!

Big thanks to Maya for taking the time to record with us!

Follow Maya on Twitter @mayaonthenet and check out her website to explore her work, mayaontheinter.net

Follow us on Twitter @waitingtosign and Instagram @waitingtobesigned

Support the show by donating to wtbs.tez or wtbs.eth or collecting our fx(text) articles

Thanks to PixelWank for the intro & outro music!
