  • A brief into of how Maya got into making art with code & releasing projects as NFTs
  • Getting exposed to astrology in LA and the online structures of identity
  • The logistics of building "I'm Feeling Lucky" and how it relates to "Fake It 'Till You Make It"
  • The personal aspect of "Read It And Weep"
  • Cyborgs and feminism
  • Growing up online, the fear + shame of posting, and the performance of 'self' on the internet
  • How astrology and generative art overlap
  • Finding comfort in simulation theory and determinism
  • Humor in art and the internet
  • Rapid Fire Questions!

Big thanks to Maya for taking the time to record with us!

Follow Maya on Twitter @mayaonthenet and check out her website to explore her work, mayaontheinter.net

Follow us on Twitter @waitingtosign and Instagram @waitingtobesigned

Support the show by donating to wtbs.tez or wtbs.eth or collecting our fx(text) articles

Thanks to PixelWank for the intro & outro music!

item.title: E83 - Endless Fluid Process
item.description:
E83 of WTBS with special co-host Jeres! This episode was recorded on August 31, 2023 and released September 4, 2023. Thanks to Jeres for sticking around to record the whole show with us and congrats on the upcoming Art Blocks Curated release.

In this episode we discuss:

  • Mini interview with Jeres about "Torrent", their upcoming AB Curated release. We talk about the inspiration and process for creating the project, how Jeres feels about using animation in their work, the thrill of being accepted as a Curated release and what it is like working with the AB board on a drop.
  • fx(hash) poll discussion: What are the pros and cons of allowing pre-rendered work on fx(hash)?
  • Will pick's up a Maya Man 'Shift Reality Instantly' on fx(hash)
  • Shoutouts to The Way Of Thinking by Collapsar Beauty and September Mond by Holger Lippmann, two very mintable projects on fx(hash)

Follow Jeres on Twitter @heyjeres and please do check out Torrent this week if you have the eth to mint!

Follow us on Twitter @waitingtosign and on instagram @waitingtobesigned

Support the show by minting our articles on fx(text) or donating directly to wtbs.tez & wtbs.eth

Episode Art: Sinuosity #145 by Jeres

Intro and Outro music by Pixelwank

item.title: A Different Kind Of Nerd: Interview with Artnome (Jason Bailey)
item.description:
Will & Trinity are very excited to share this interview with Jason Bailey AKA Artnome. Jason is a long-time NFT collector and one of the earliest advocates for art on the blockchain. He is the founder of RightClickSave and ClubNFT, platforms that are helping to educate collectors on the history and preservation of digital art. In this episode we discuss:

  • Jason's background in art and collecting, and how he came to discover NFTs
  • The 'Artnome Effect' and the reality of collecting in public as someone with influence
  • The eras of NFT cycles from a collector POV
  • What it means to "back up" your NFT collection and why a collector should care to learn about how their tokens work and where the art actually lives
  • Advice to artists and collectors during a bear market (and whether there is any advice to give)
  • Tezos vs. Ethereum, Chain risk & platform risks during a bear market
  • What it's like being a content producer during the bear market

Huge thanks for Jason to coming on and giving us so much of his time to record. Thanks as well to Danielle for helping to get us introduced.

Episode Art: Growth v02 #326 by flight404

Support the show by donating to wtbs.tez or wtbs.eth & collecting our episode notes on fx(text)

Intro and Outro music by Pixelwank

item.title: E82 - You've Changed
item.description:
E82 recorded August 24 2023, released August 28 2023. Had a lot of recording issues on this one... Here is what we discussed:

  • Babies walking and communication
  • OpenSea giving up on royalties and caving to pressure from Blur... and still no Tezos integration !?!?!
  • Upcoming interview with Artnome and SimonSays
  • The launch of zeroone.art
  • Quasi Dragon Studies by Harvey Rayner, released on Verse
  • On Chain Summer kicks off on Highlight.xyz with projects from Melissa W, Leander Herzog, James Merril and Holger Lippman
  • Heart + Craft launches on Prohibition
  • Rhythm & The Machine by Lars Wander drops on Tonic as a slower-than-expected mint
  • Rückkopplung by pxlshrd x Tender wins the week on fx(hash) with amazing secondary action
  • UpredictableTaco is back on fx(hash) with Lonely Tree

Follow us on Twitter @waitingtosign and Instagram @waitingtobesigned

Support the show by donating to wtbs.tez or wtbs.eth, and collect our show notes on fx(text)

Episode art is Rückkopplung #172 owned by KenConsumer

Thanks to PixelWank for the intro and outro music!

item.title: E81 - Cringe Is Relative
item.description:
E81 of our weekly show, "Cringe Is Relative", recorded on August 17, 2023. In this episode we discuss:

  • The unfortunate story of Ichi passing away.
  • gen.art and eclipse.art shutting down.
  • Coinbase's "Base" L2 on Ethereum goes live with an emphasis on art NFTs.
  • Heart + Craft, an "inclusive" edition collaborative project between Snowfro and Prohibition.art
  • Digits by Loren Bednar, surprise fx(hash) release
  • Remnants of Humanity by Kira0 on Alba
  • 923 Empty Rooms by Casey Reas, Bright Moments x Art Blocks
  • SPACE\TIME by Loackme released on Verse
  • KRILLER.COM releases an album on Spotify
  • Tons to look ahead to, including Quasi Dragon Studies on Verse and Rhythm & The Machine on Tonic!

Follow us on Twitter @waitingtosign and Instagram @waitingtobesigned

Support the show by donating to wtbs.tez or wtbs.eth & collecting our episode notes on fx(text)

Intro and outro music by Pixelwank

item.title: E80 - Love, Light & Shilling
item.description:
Welcome to your weekly gen-art recap episode. This week we discuss:

  • fx(hash) funding announcement, our interview with Ciphrd, and deleted tweets
  • Our guesses and takes of Proof Grails season 4.
  • Fold by Rudxane finally releases to huge success (and Blur farming)
  • Rebellion by Melissa Wiederrecht and the return of surprise drops on fx(hash)
  • The massive success of the Zach Lieberman exhibition on Verse
  • The Unit London exhibition that features a few artists we know from fx(hash) + the associated drops from Marcelo Rodriguez on fx(hash)
  • Shout Outs to Rich Poole for Manu and Gabriel for Follow My Lead

item.title: Unlocking A Generative Future: Interview with Ciphrd
item.description:
Welcome to our third interview episode with Ciphrd, the creator of fx(hash). In this episode we discuss the the recently announced funding that will be used to expand fx(hash) to the Ethereum blockchain along with other features slated for the fx(hash) 2.0 release. We also get Ciphrd's take on the current generative art NFT ecosystem, the recent influx of new platforms, and ideas for future-proofing fx(hash) for the next bear market. We hope you enjoy this one as much as we did. Big thanks to Ciphrd and the team for coordinating this episode with us.

Episode Art: RGB #1 by Ciphrd

Follow Ciphrd and fx(hash) on Twitter and join the fx(hash) Discord for access to the amazing community there.

Follow us on Twitter @waitingtosign and Instagram @waitingtobesigned

Intro & Outro music by Pixelwank

item.title: E79 - Back From Hiatus
item.description:
Will & Trinity are back from ~5 weeks off and ready to recap the latest events in generative art. In this episode we discuss:

  • The interviews we released in July + one yet to drop
  • The Prohibition launch + platform roundup!
  • The Jackson Pollock NFT thing
  • Tez Etherlink & Metamask integration + how little we understand about all this
  • Monthly Market Update!
  • Will got a Frame + Pickups!
  • The Vera Molnar release with Sotheby's

  • Interference by Phaust (Tonic)

  • TVMOIF by Thomas Noya (fxhash)

  • Liminal Rooms by Lisa Orth (Alba)

item.title: Living With Images: Interview with Zach Lieberman
item.description:
Another hot interview to end the August podcast hiatus. Will & Trinity had the excellent opportunity to sit down with Zach Lieberman just ahead of his Verse Solos exhibition, Studies in Color, Light and Geometry. We talk to Zach about using the blockchain to preserve art, what he looks for when curating work for release, the challenges of long form, the importance of prints and much more. We hope you enjoy this episode. Big thanks to Zach for taking the time to record with us!

Follow Zach on Twitter @zachlieberman and instagram to check out all the amazing works in progress he shares.

Follow us on Twitter @waitingtosign and instagram @waitingtobesigned

Consider donating to support the show: wtbs.eth and wtbs.tez

Intro and Outro music by Pixelwank

item.title: Creating Decentralized Art: Interview with Harvey Rayner
item.description:
Trinity & Will sit down with Harvey Rayner for a special interview episode. We dig into Harvey's background in art and coding, obsession with geometry, generative color, and his upcoming Verse Solos exhibition, Quasi Dragon Studies. It was particularly fun for us to discuss the unique release and building components of QDS, Harvey's hopes and fears for the project, and a nice detour into our shared passion for bouldering :)

Big thanks to Harvey for coming on the show and to the Verse team for helping bring us together.

Follow Harvey on Twitter @harvey_rayner and check out his other work at pattern.co

Follow us on Twitter @waitingtosign and consider donating to the show at wtbs.tez or wtbs.eth

item.title: The Technology of Poetry: Interview with Sasha Stiles & Nathaniel Stern
item.description:
Will & Trinity sit down with creative partners Sasha Stiles (sashastiles.com) & Nathaniel Stern (nathanielstern.com) for an epic interview. In this episode we discuss:

  • The creative background of both artists and how they came to become collaborators
  • Nathaniel's "The World After Us" show
  • Sasha's relationship with A.I. as a collaborator and student
  • What is poetry and how is it influenced by new media disciplines
  • Using Midjourney & Stable Diffusion & the issues and appeal of prompt-based systems
  • Mother Computer & exploring the relationship between artificial intelligence and natural intelligence
  • A.I. Language models and entering the era of the next great language leap\
  • The challenges of selling ambitious work in the NFT ecosystem & the forces of value
  • The story of 0RAL B1N4RY on fx(hash)
  • Pushing back on what "generative art" looks like
  • The potential of fx(params)
  • Sci-Fi recommendations!

Follow Sasha on Twitter @sashastiles and follow Nathaniel @nathanielstern

Check out (and consider minting) their Art Blocks Curated release Still Moving

Find their Tezos work on fx(hash) here and here and on OBJKT here and here

Follow us on Twitter @waitingtosign and Instagram/Threads @waitingtobesigned

Consider supporting the show by donating to wtbs.tez or wtbs.eth

item.title: E78 - Goodbye For July
item.description:
WTBS is taking a brief summer break to rest, relax, and reinvigorate the show! We will still be releasing interviews at our usual cadence & our next planned weekly episode will be for August 8. A little rundown of what we discuss in this episode:

  • The July Hiatus
  • fx(hash) 2.0 announcement... ETH is coming
  • On-Chain vs. IPFS and the potential implications for fx(hash) 2.0
  • Vera Molnar long-form generative release on Sotheby's
  • Quarterly Market Update!

  • Spazio/Colore drop on Alba

item.title: Digital. Mechanical. Kinetic. Materialized: Interview with Andreas Gysin
item.description:
We are excited to share this interview with Andreas Gysin with you on the same day that Verse debuts their first Solos exhibition with him and his creative partner Sidi Vanetti. Their show, Materialized, showcases a variety of physical pieces along with a new long-form generative project Wayfinder. In this interview we learn the origin of the partnership between Andreas and Sidi, their obsession with restrictions, the semiotics of ascii art, and plenty more.

Learn more about Andreas and Sidi on their website

Follow Andreas on Twitter @andreasgysin and check out his personal website as well https://ertdfgcvb.xyz/

Twitter: @WaitingToSign

Instagram: @waitingtobesigned

Donations: wtbs.tez && wtbs.eth

Intro music by The Gas Station, as heard in *Sunset Dancers* by Laurean0

Outro music "Always Be Listed" by Pixelwank

item.title: E77 - "Cue the Olivia"
item.description:
Good friends, good art, good audio, good times. This week your hosts are "getting physical" with the launch of redeemable tokens on fx(hash)! In this episode we discuss, in roughly this order:

  • Car trips and the Otherworld podcast
  • Redeemable tokens on fx(hash), the launch of fx(Versa) ++ Le Random
  • A little more platform talk, which is our right!
  • Big success with Alba's first week of the Flatlands exhibition
  • Trinity shills the AB Curated Dopamine Machines by Steve Pikelny
  • Nuages possibles by Joanie Lemercier
  • Who is Fakewhale and what we liked about their curated release on objkt
  • Somewhere in Between by GorillaSun && Si-operator by HAL09999, our two favorites from the Proof of X event releases
  • Machine-hearted by Ivona Tau, her first long form AI project (that we can think of)
  • “Simple Thoughts” exhibition by Khwampa on Verse, insane MS Paint art!

Twitter: @WaitingToSign

Instagram: @waitingtobesigned

Donations: wtbs.tez && wtbs.eth

Episode Art: Folds within Folds by Khwampa

Intro music by The Gas Station, as heard in *Sunset Dancers* by Laurean0

item.title: E76 - Minimum Viable Edit
item.description:
Recording a little early this week with both hosts away for the weekend... this one is a little looser and longer but excellent discussion as always! In this episode we discuss:


  • Weekend plans + the possible connection between the Impressionist movement and the current moment in generative art
  • The Goose sale at Sotheby's
  • More open platforms on eth and whether there is real value in being on-chain
  • The latest Generative Architecture exhibition on Verse and how it played out, with specific discussion of the projects Draftwerk, ODA, Tekton: Optima, Funktor, Yume no Sekai
  • DOM1 by Leander Herzog, Milian Mori, and HEK
  • Shoutouts to M4LF0 by poperbu and the series of projects being released by TGAM

Twitter: @WaitingToSign

Instagram: @waitingtobesigned

Donations: wtbs.tez && wtbs.eth

Intro music by The Gas Station, as heard in *Sunset Dancers* by Laurean0

item.dc:creator: Willpop + Trinity
item.itunes:summary:
Recording a little early this week with both hosts away for the weekend... this one is a little looser and longer but excellent discussion as always! In this episode we discuss:


  • Weekend plans + the possible connection between the Impressionist movement and the current moment in generative art
  • The Goose sale at Sotheby's
  • More open platforms on eth and whether there is real value in being on-chain
  • The latest Generative Architecture exhibition on Verse and how it played out, with specific discussion of the projects Draftwerk, ODA, Tekton: Optima, Funktor, Yume no Sekai
  • DOM1 by Leander Herzog, Milian Mori, and HEK
  • Shoutouts to M4LF0 by poperbu and the series of projects being released by TGAM

Twitter: @WaitingToSign

Instagram: @waitingtobesigned

item.title: Telling the Story of Generative Art: Interview with MonkAntony
item.description:
This week we dive deep into the history of generative art with the legendary MonkAntony AKA Peter Bauman. You might know Monk from his early presence collecting on fx(hash), or his writing on Right Click Save. More recently he has joined the team at Le Random, working with them to build out the massive timeline of Everything related to generative art. In this episode we discuss:

  • Monk's history in art and NFTs
  • Monk's philosophy for collecting and how that as evolved as he has expanded his knowledge through research.
  • How Monk's initial writing for Tender landed him a role at Le Random
  • What IS Le Random, how do they collect and what are their goals?
  • Writing and researching the 1,000 moments that make up the Generative Art Timeline
  • The origin of the MonkAntony name, how to say it, and other Rapid Fire questions.

Follow MonkAntony on Twitter @monkantony_tez and check out his linktree to explore his writing

Follow us on Twitter @waitingtosign and on Instagram @waitingtobesigned

Donate! wtbs.tez and wtbs.eth

Episode Art: Farbteiler #45 by Erik Swahn

item.title: A New Art Movement: Interview with Valérie Whitacre, Head of Art at TriliTech
item.description:
The WTBS crew is extremely excited to bring you this interview with Valérie Whitacre, Head of Art at Trilitech. As Tezos enthusiasts and collectors, we know surprisingly little about what is going on with the teams that are in charge of shepherding our beloved ecosystem into the future. Valérie, in her role at TriliTech, is responsible for growing the "Art Vertical" on Tezos and the ideal interview guest to bring us up to speed on everything that is going on behind the scenes on the Tezos side. In this episode we discuss:

  • Valérie's history in the legacy art world and how she came to TriliTech
  • What IS TriliTech and what are their goals with art and Tezos?
  • The significance of the New Art Movement we are experiencing.
  • The challenges of the legacy art world and their resistance to web3
  • the value of infinitely reproducible art.
  • The virtues of Tezos as a Layer 1 chain!
  • Who should we interview next??

Episode Art: VERI - WAC Lab Season 2 Demo Day

Follow TriliTech on Twitter @trilitech

Follow us on Twitter @waitingtosign and Instagram @waitingtobesigned

Support the show by donating! wtbs.tez & wtbs.eth

item.title: Every Day Obsession: Interview with Cory Haber
item.description:
Our first-ever interview with an artist who has not dropped on fx(hash)... yet. We hope enjoy this conversation with Cory, recorded a week before the release of Atmospheres on Verse. Atmospheres is Cory's follow-up to SOL, the project that first put him on our radar. We enjoyed digging into Cory's history in art, his shift to coding, his passion for building & plotting, and the stories of SOL and Atmospheres. We also learned Cory's current evaluation of the market, his philosophy on releasing projects, and more. Huge thanks to Cory for taking the time to come on the show and sharing so candidly about his art and process.

Follow Cory on Twitter (but don't expect him to tweet) @Cory_Haber

Check out Atmospheres dropping on Verse tomorrow!

Check out Cory's work on Foundation and OpenSea

Twitter: @WaitingToSign

Instagram: @waitingtobesigned

Donations: wtbs.tez & wtbs.eth

Intro music by The Gas Station, as heard in Sunset Dancers by Laurean0

Outro music Always Be Listed by Pixelwank

item.title: Peak Bright Moments: Interview with Seth Goldstein
item.description:
Will and Trinity interview Seth from Bright Moments just days before the start of their massive Tokyo event. In this episode we learn about Seth's background in art and technology, the early days of Bright Moments and the culture of Venice Beach, the importance of live events and physical friction in collecting art, the future of Bright Moments, the health of the market and a lot more. Huge thanks to Seth for taking the time to talk to us all the way from Tokyo, we are thrilled to share this episode with you.

Follow Seth on Twitter @seth and follow Bright Moments on Twitter @brtmoments

Learn more about Bright Moments Tokyo and everything else BM has to offer (past and future)

Follow us on Twitter @waitingtosign

Donate! wtbs.tez && wtbs.eth

item.title: Imperfect Perfectionism: Interview with Rudxane
item.description:
WTBS is back with another interview episode, this time with fx(hash) legend Rudxane! We've been fans of Rudxane for as long as we have been collecting generative art. In this episode we talk about the humanity of imperfection in art, the virtues of releasing on other chains/platforms (including Bitcoin), long lost collaborations, what to expect in 2023, going "physical", the bear market shakeout + more! Big thanks to Rudxane for coming on the show.

Follow Rudxane on Twitter @rudxane1 and check out his website for info on all his work.

Follow us Twitter: @WaitingToSign

Follow us Instagram: @waitingtobesigned

Send us a Donation: wtbs.tez && wtbs.eth

item.description:
Live from New York it's WTBS! This week was all about getting out of the house and touching grass (or concrete, in our case). NFT NYC landed in Will & Trinity's backyard! Trinity was on a panel, Will got a Live Mint, Zancan proposed to Ozzie, and the pizza was amazing. item.title: Rabbit-Hole of Passion: Interview with Lonliboy
item.description:
Lonliboy joins the WTBS crew for our biggest interview episode yet! We dive deep into the Lonliboy story and explore his deep passion for ART. We discuss the challenges of managing a collection in public, the current reckoning of NFT art and platforms, the perils of taxes, some of his plans at Verse, and more. Huge thanks to Lonli for coming on the show and sharing so openly and honestly.

follow Lonli on twitter @chilltulpa

follow us on twitter @waitingtosign

follow us on instagram @waitingtobesigned

Consider donating! wtbs.tez, wtbs.eth

item.title: Brute Forcing Beauty: Interview with Jeres
item.description:
It's finally time... Jeres is on the show in their first ever podcast appearance! We are so excited to share this interview with our longtime (in crypto terms) friend and WTBS token collaborator. In this interview we go over the history of Jeres' work on fx(hash), near-breakthrough moments, what it means when a project begins with a capital letter, finding emotion in art, the virtues of publishing under multiple accounts, and more.


Follow Jeres on Twitter @HeyJeres and check out all their great work on fx(hash)


Follow us on Twitter: @WaitingToSign

Instagram: @waitingtobesigned

Donations: wtbs.tez, wtbs.eth

Episode Art: Here, After #82 by Jeres


Intro music by The Gas Station as heard in Sunset Dancers by Laurean0

item.description:
Generative & AI artist Thomas Noya joins Will and Trinity for a classic WTBS interview episode. Thomas has been working quietly for months collaborating with us on our next fx(hash) token release, which we are excited to announce here! We also get to know more about Thomas and his background in art, how he trains his models, his plans for 2023, and deep dives into many of his amazing projects!

item.title: E60 - Royalties & Loyalties
item.description:
Another discussion-heavy episode this week. It's impossible to avoid the conversation around creator royalties and what platforms like Blur are doing to the Ethereum ecosystem. While art seems to be somewhat insulated (especially here on Tezos) we need to prepare ourselves for the inevitability of "royalty optional" trading in the future. 

We also have our weekly market update, new drops from fx(hash) and verse.works, and shout outs! 

Twitter: @WaitingToSign

Instagram: @waitingtobesigned

Donations: waitingtosign.tez, wtbs.tez, wtbs.eth

Smooth Steps, Andreas Gysin

re:build, Chris McCully

asunder, pxlshrd

Fuga a tientas, Alejandro Campos

Texture Part II, Olga Fradina

Outro music "Always Be Listed" by pixelwank

item.title: Legitimizing The Space: Interview with Jamie Gourlay
item.description:
Jamie Gourlay is co-founder of verse.works, a curated digital art collecting platform that has featured many artists we know and love from fx(hash). In our longest interview yet, Will and Trinity dive into Jamie's background in the traditional art world, the challenges of NFTs as a brand, and a long discussion on the importance of markets and how to legitimize the space to make it more appealing to new collectors. This episode is especially timely considering the renewed debate about creator royalties and the non-enforcement policy just announced by OpenSea.

Follow Jamie on Twitter @jamiegourlay and follow verse.work on Twitter @verse_works

Check out the latest verse.works exhibition, Imperfections

Twitter: @WaitingToSign

Instagram: @waitingtobesigned

Donations: waitingtosign.tez, wtbs.eth

item.title: Information Asymmetries: Interview with Kyloren
item.description:
Another legendary collector joins us for over an hour of market and collecting talk. Kylo was one of the first to mint a Squiggle on Art Blocks and was one of the earliest big collectors on fx(hash) as well. We talk to him about his general thesis for collecting generative art NFTs, what is was like back in the "old days" of fx(hash), why he is starting his ALFA fund, and plenty of artist and project talk! Big thanks to Kylo for making the time and joining us from half way around the world to get this episode recorded.

Follow Kylo on Twitter @Kyloren_NFT and check out his ALFA Fund project

Twitter: @WaitingToSign

Instagram: @waitingtobesigned

Donations: waitingtosign.tez, waitingtosign.eth

Intro music by The Gas Station, as heard in *Sunset Dancers* by Laurean0

Outro music by Nor44, as heard in My Demons

item.title: What Is Long-Form Generative A.I. Art?: Interview with Emil Corsillo & Ira Greenberg
item.description:
Will and Trinity sit down with Emil and Ira (two of the five founders of emprops.ai, the long-form generative AI platform) to learn about the origins of Emergent Properties and answer the burning question: What is long-form generative A.I.??? Ira fills us in on how he made The Oracles, the smash-hit "genesis" project of the platform, and we get a peak at the process behind Emil's just-released project, Ghost in the Machine.

We also chat them up about their own extensive histories in art, their work and collecting on fxhash, and music recommendations! 

Follow Emergent Properties on Twitter @emprops_io

Follow Emil on Twitter @3mil_c and check out his work on fx(hash)

Follow Ira on Twitter @iragreenberg and check out his work on fx(hash) as well

Twitter: @WaitingToSign

Instagram: @waitingtobesigned

Donations: waitingtosign.tez

Intro music by The Gas Station, as heard in *Sunset Dancers* by Laurean0

Outro music by Nor44, as heard in My Demons

item.title: E51 - 2022 in Review, 2023 in the Queue
item.description:
2022 is about the end, so we are Signing the Year by reviewing the highs and lows of fx(hash), our personal top 10 projects, and a healthy dose of introspection about making the show. This one gets meta folks, hope you enjoy the candid conversation about our journey creating content for an entire year, and what we are looking forward to in 2023. Thank you as always to everyone who has taken the time to listen and support the show, we definitely would not have kept with it without your friendship and encouragement. 

PLEASE Check out the fxtext article for this one they are jam packed with all of the projects and events discussed!

Twitter: @WaitingToSign

Instagram: @waitingtobesigned

Donations: waitingtosign.tez, waitingtosign.eth, waitingtobesigned.eth

item.title: Tuning The Frequency: Interview with Chris McCully
item.description:
Chris McCully veteran fx(hash) creator, joins the WTBS crew in a special interview episode. Chris talks to us about his background in film and design and how it shaped his early success on the platform. We hear more about Bedlam, Chris's original "magnum opus", and go deep on some of his popular recent releases. Chris also gives us a HEN history lesson! Truly something for everyone in this one.

Follow Chris on Twitter: @SpinkDinky

Check out Chris's work on fx(hash)

item.title: What Landlines Art Is: Interview with Landlines Art
item.description:
As 2022 quickly comes to a close, we could not be more excited to bring you an interview with Landlines Art. Barely an episode passes where we do not reference at least one Landlines project, so suffice to say we are huge fans of his work. We cover a broad range of topics with some especially interesting conversation about how Landlines thinks about edition sizing and the market, and how fx(params) will play into future releases.

Plus, a music recommendation! 

Follow Landlines on Twitter @landlinesart1

Check out Landlines' fx(hash) work as Landlines Art and Extracurricular Activites

Twitter: @WaitingToSign

Instagram: @waitingtobesigned

Donations: waitingtosign.tez

Episode Art: Absolute Error #17

Intro music by The Gas Station, as heard in *Sunset Dancers* by Laurean0

Outro music by Nor44, as heard in My Demons

item.title: The First Year of fx(hash): Interview with ciphrd
item.description:
WTBS is celebrating a year of fx(hash) with platform founder, and returning guest, ciphrd. We look back at the how the some of the features from 1.0 have played out so far, talk about the greater crypto/NFT market, and then look ahead at what's to come still for fx(hash) in year 2. We are very excited and intrigued by fx(params) and the economics behind mint passes!

To support the show, checkout the transcript on fxtext and consider collecting as a sign of love and support :)

Follow ciphrd on Twitter @ciphrd

Follow fx(hash) on Twitter @fx_hash_

Twitter: @WaitingToSign

Instagram: @waitingtobesigned

Donations: waitingtosign.tez

Episode Art: FXHASH Generative Logo #198 by fxhash

Intro music by The Gas Station, as heard in *Sunset Dancers* by Laurean0

Outro music by Nor44, as heard in My Demons

item.title: E45 - Sweeps Week
item.description:
Bad week for crypto but huge week for fxhash! Tez is cheap(er) and multiple projects saw huge runs on the secondary. We also saw the release of Acequia, which at the time of this episode going up has broken into the top 15 volume of all time on the market. Follow along via our fxtext article, and thank you also to everyone who donated and purchased our show notes on fxhash! 

Twitter: @WaitingToSign

Instagram: @waitingtobesigned

Donations: waitingtosign.tez

Episode Art: Acequia #343 by Rich Poole and ThePaperCrane

Sweeps!

Kim Asendorf: Tr4ns4ctions + some RAB / Monogrids

msoriaro: Contras and more Toccata, el inefable momento

Erik Swahn: Farbteiler Kinetograms Designatori Retrogrades

Dragons

Infinite Scaling System by LostPixel

Projects of the Week:

Floating Medusas, quentinhocde

ROIL, KilledByAPixel

Acequia, Rich Poole x ThePaperCrane

Imperium, Mr. Mallory 

Shoutouts:

Departure Within Shattered Windmills, Eric Davidson

Simulated Reality, ge1doot

Bleached, Kunio Okuizumi

Intro music by The Gas Station, as heard in *Sunset Dancers* by Laurean0

Outro music by Nor44, as heard in My Demons

item.title: Building The Museum: Interview with LeMonde2D
item.description:
WTBS is bringing you another special collector interview! We sit down with Didi aka Lemonde2d and walk through his whirlwind history collecting generative art on fxhash. In this episode we discuss building the ultimate GenArt Museum, why he goes so deep on some collections, and the importance of price (or lack thereof).

Check out The Museum on Deca to see Didi's amazing collection

Follow Didi on Twitter @lemonde2d

Follow us on Twitter @waitingtosign

Follow us on Instagram @waitingtobesigned

Kindly accepting your donations at waitingtosign.tez

Artists and Collections referenced:

Little Trees by Brian Gawlik

el inefable momento by msoriaro

Bardez by Nat Sarkissian

Toccata by msoriaro and Andreas Rau

Tr4ns4ctions by Kim Asendorf

Ethereal Microcosm by ciphrd

RGB by ciphrd

Landlines Landlines Landlines

Monoliths by Orr Kislev and Ganchitecture

Millefoglie by Stefano Contiero

eltono

Turner Light by ileivoivm 

horizon(te)s by Iskra Velitchkova and Zack Lieberman 

qubibi and leftovers

Emotional Shell by wwatkins and Tender

item.title: Always Moving Forward: Interview with Lisa Orth
item.description:
The day has finally come... WTBS sits down (virtually) with the legend herself, Lisa Orth. In this episode we dive into Lisa's story and her history with art, the origin of her signature color palette, any why Tezos is punk. This conversation is especially rich with life lessons and perspective on how to live a truly passionate life, we hope you enjoy!

Links:

Lesbian Mayor: The Lisa Orth Story documentary on YouTube

The playlist Lisa listened too that inspired her classic color palette!

Lisa Orth on fxhash

Lisa Orth Sketchbook on fxhash

The Problem of Crypto Art by Joanie Lermercier

Follow Lisa on Twitter @lisaorthstudio

Follow us on Twitter: @WaitingToSign

Instagram: @waitingtobesigned

Donations: waitingtosign.tez

Episode Art Between Stations #88 by Lisa Orth, owned by Trinity

item.title: Hooked on Art: Interview with AJBerni
item.description:
Will and Trinity sit down with AJBerni to learn the story of TENDER. Adam shares his background in art and design, how Tender collaborations work, the decision to sell the Tender Pass, and what we have to look forward to over the second half of the year. 

If you haven't already, check out Tender.art to explore the Icons list and the many other publicly available features.

item.title: E29 - Intro to Psychology and Marketing
item.description:
Trinity is back from the cabin! In this episode we discuss gmDAO drops, the shvmbldr situation, and Will's journey into 1/1 collecting. We also take an interesting diversion during the Drops of the Week to explore the psychological effects of Dutch Auctions, project floors, and trading tez for hype.

item.title: Exploring In-Between States: Interview with Ivona Tau, PhD
item.description:
Will and Trinity have a virtual sit-down with Dr. Ivona Tau for some serious AI/GAN art education. Ivona walks us through the basics of training a GAN system and how she uses them in her work... and from there the conversation goes a bit more free-form discussing publicly available tools like Midjourney, misconceptions about AI art, her experiences in the traditional art world, and more. 

Follow Ivona on Twitter @ivonatau

Learn more about Ivona and her work on her website https://ivonatau.com/

Twitter: @WaitingToSign

Instagram: @waitingtobesigned

Donations: waitingtosign.tez

Intro music by The Gas Station, as heard in Sunset Dancers by Laurean0

Outro music by Nor44

item.title: Buying Through the Bear Market: Interview with KRPDM DAO
item.description:
Trinity and Will interview Ed, one of the founding members of the KRPDM (Carpe Diem). KRPDM is a Decentralized Autonomous Organization [DAO] that collects NFTs from all corners of the Tezos eco-system. From the outset they've had a clear directive to collect gen-art, and see fx(hash) as cultural epicenter for long form gen-art on Tezos. 

In this episode we discuss the logistics of collecting as an organization, optimism about the Tezos blockchain, the DAO's bullishness despite the impending bear market, and of course, THE ART.

Follow KRPDM on Twitter @KRP_DM

Follow us on Twitter @WaitingToSign

Instagram @waitingtobesigned

Donations waitingtosign.tez

item.title: The Importance of Color: Interview with Anna Lucia
item.description:
Trinity and Will are joined by the fxhash master of murals, Anna Lucia! Huge thank to Anna for taking the time to join us for this interview.

Follow Anna on Twitter: @annaluciacodes

Follow Anna on Instagram: @anna.lucia.codes

https://y.at/%F0%9F%8E%B2%F0%9F%96%BC%F0%9F%92%BE%F0%9F%90%89

Follow us on Twitter: @WaitingToSign

Instagram: @waitingtobesigned

Donations: waitingtosign.tez

Topics include:

-Anna's history on art, coding, and general love for math

-The process behind her two fxhash drops, Art For Walls In Public Spaces and Perpetual Oscillations

-The importance of experimenting with color and making your own palettes

-Anna's involvement in The Blind Gallery (and a potential next fxhash drop)

-What Anna likes to collect on fxhash

-A hint about Anna's next project on Art Blocks

-What is a YAT??

-Childhood math games

-ANNA IS DUTCH!

Theme music is by The Gas Station, as heard in Sunset Dancers by Laurean0

item.title: Playing Code Golf: Interview with Piter Pasma
item.description:
Special bonus interview with Piter Pasma! Piter has been making art with code for decades, but you may know him best for the work he has released on fxhash (Hypergiraffe, Dot Produkt, Angry Noise, Gonzion) and Art Blocks (Skulptuur). In this episode we learn about about Piter's history in code and code-based art, the origins of his long-form generative works, the beauty of code optimization, and the challenges of releasing art as NFTs. Please enjoy!

https://piterpasma.nl/

The Essence of Linear Algebra Youtube Series

Follow Piter on twitter @piterpasma

Follow us on twitter @waitingtosign

Follow us on instagram @waitingtobesigned

Support the show via donations to our tez wallet, waitingtosign.tez

item.title: Interview w/ ClownVamp
item.description:
Will and Trinity talk with ClownVamp, new-ish and notorious-ish collector on fxhash. We learn a bit about his background in NFTs, philosophy on collecting and flipping, Pokemon card pack-busting, our favorite artists on fxhash, the impact of royalties on secondary volume and the impending eth collector invasion. Plus the origin of the ClownVamp name! This episode is PACKED.

Follow ClownVamp on twitter @ClownVamp

Check out Vamp's amazing OnCyber gallery

Follow us on twitter @waitingtosign

Follow us on instagram @waitingtobesigned

Support the show by donating to our Tezos wallet, waitingtosign.tez

Theme music is by The Gas Station, as heard in Sunset Dancers by Laurean0

item.title: Interview with Amy Goodchild!
item.description:
Amy Goodchild comes by for a conversation about the intersection of Art and Code. In this interview we discuss: 

  • Amy's background in art, installation and design
  • Amy's Maplands project and blog post
  • The balance between aesthetics and code in evaluating generative art and the importance of education in both
  • Midjourney and whether Will should be scared of it or not

Follow Amy on Twitter @amygoodchild

Follow Amy's work and writing at amygoodchild.com

Amy's Maplands write-up 

Amy's essay "What Is Generative Art?"

Learn to code with Dan Shiffman's The Coding Train on Youtube

Follow us on twitter @WaitingToSign

Follow us on Instagram @WaitingToBeSigned

Donations graciously accepted at waitingtosign.tez

Hopefully you enjoy getting to learn more about us while we wait for the 1.0 launch!

item.title: Ciphrd End of Beta Interview
item.description:
That's right folks the man himself Ciphrd is on the show for this special bonus interview episode. Absolutely thrilled to have the creator of fx(hash) join us to discuss fx(hash) present and future. An absolute must listen episode.

Some topics we cover:

-Ciphrd's history in generative art and crypto

-New features coming in the next iteration of the contract

-Curation, discovery, and fx(hash) as a vehicle for learning about generative art

-Governance, the DAO, and the delay in the fx(hash) token launch

-Ciphrd's goals for fx(hash)

item.title: Interview w/ Kaloh
item.description:
Willpop and Trinity interview Kaloh, the prolific writer known for his work covering the NFT space at kaloh.xyz. We discuss Kaloh's recent fxhash genesis project, Impossible Sneakers, the current state of the fxhash market + where we might be headed, the impact of bots on the health of the platform, what makes an artist successful (and some of our picks for who is doing it right), and look ahead to what is coming as we leave the beta behind. 

Follow Kaloh: @Kaloh_NFT

Twitter: @WaitingToSign

Instagram: @waitingtobesigned

Donations: waitingtosign.tez

Theme music is by The Gas Station, as heard in Sunset Dancers by Laurean0

item.title: Interview w/ MJLindow
item.description:
Willpop and Trinity interview MJLindow, the artist behind Jardin, Waiting in Afton, Beyond, Lepidoptera, Stippled Impressions, Caught In A Void and Kunza. We talk about his path to becoming a generative artist, success, expectations, goals, and get extremely rough estimate of when his next project may release on fxhash.

Thank you to all who submitted questions, sorry if we did not shout you out specifically during the show.

Follow us on twitter: @WaitingToSign

Follow Lindow on twitter: @MJLindow

If you love the content and are inspired to donate, our donation wallet is waitingtosign.tez

item.title: Investigating Clews: Interview with abstractment
item.description:
Trinity and WillPOP interview abstractment, the artist behind the breakthrough Clew project and the #clewcrew.

Abstractment (Preston) fills us in on some of his global background and history with art, his journey as a collector and coder, and how he found success as a new creator on fxhash.

PLUS: *exclusive* info on the next abstractment release!

Follow Preston on twitter @abstractment (and here is one of a few threads explaining future airdrops)

Check out his website for a beautiful, detailed look into Clew.

Preston is currently living in Thailand, please get in touch with him if you are a part of the local Thai NFT/Generative Art scene

item.title: Breaking G L A S S: Interview with KenConsumer
item.description:
Our first interview with KenConsumer from the Discord community. Huge thanks to Ken for agreeing to come on the show and talk candidly about his experience in the traditional art world and his approach to collecting. See links below to learn more about Ken and his amazing history!

Follow Ken on Twitter: @KenconsumerE

Follow us on Twitter: @waitingtosign

Support us via donations: waitingtosign.tez


More of Ken's links:

sp2.berlin - Ken's gallery

kenconsumer.wordpress.com - Ken's art

https://hicetnunc.art/kenconsumer - Ken's art on HEN

keithfem.com - Ken's radio station

https://www.keithbarberlin.de/ - Ken's bar if you are ever in Berlin!

item.title: A Philosophy on Flipping
item.description:
Trying something different this week with a shorter, discussion-based bonus episode. Our current thinking and approach to portfolio and wallet management has been a topic for discussion the past two weeks, but has been cut to keep our (already) lengthy weekly rundown episodes from spilling towards 3 hours. Hope everyone enjoys this candid conversation about where we are at right now as collectors, and as always, your feedback + comments are welcome via twitter or Discord.

twitter: @WaitingToSign

Donations: waitingtosign.tez

item.title: E0 - The Genesis Drop
item.description:
The very first (0th) episode of Waiting to Be Signed

Hosted by Will__Pop and Trinity

Thank you everyone for the encouragement and hope you enjoy. On this episode we introduce ourselves, and the show, to the fx(hash) community, and then jump into a brief history of the platform. We discuss the "flipper" controversy, and then recap the key drops and market action of the last week of 2021!
item.dc:creator: Willpop + Trinity
